<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478614 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: Creating a concept modeling project

- page_id: `170478614`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478614/Creating+a+concept+modeling+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:08.072+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage
- labels: ['concept-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Shortcut Keys

64854118

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Shortcut Keys

64854120

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Shortcut Keys

64854119

To create a concept modeling project

1. Click File > New Project . The New Project dialog will open.
2. Select Concept Modeling Project .
3. Name your project and select your Project location .
4. Click OK .

A new Concept Modeling diagram will open, complete with the Concept Modeling diagram palette. This diagram and its palette will also open whenever you create a new Concept Modeling diagram. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The Concept Modeling diagram.**

#### INFO: Containment Tree

Containment Tree

You can display or hide concept modeler profiles as auxiliary resources in the Containment tree by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting **Show Auxiliary Resources**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Setting concept modeler profiles as auxiliary resources to be displayed in the Containment tree.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Concept modeler auxiliary resources are displayed in grey in the Containment tree.**

#### INFO: Available From 19.0 SP1

Subproperties

GreenAvailable From 19.0 SP1

You can view subproperties in the **Subproperties Tree.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can adjust the visibility of your subproperties using the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button as shown below. By default, **Show Applied Stereotypes** is selected. The figure below shows what happens when you set each option to **True**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Simple Properties are properties that do not have any subproperties attached to them. So, if you would like to see a simple property, do not select Hide Simple Properties.

You can also toggle the subproperties tree using the diagram pane. Right-click on any property in your diagram pane and select **Concept Modeling** > **Select in** **Subproperties Tree.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also toggle the subproperties tree using the **Main Menu.** Select **Window** > **Subproperties.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### INFO: Shortcut Keys

Shortcut Keys

The following table shows the buttons in the Concept Modeling diagram palette which represent the elements you can use to create a Concept Modeling diagram. You can drag the button to a diagram to create that type of element. The shortcut key may also make it easier for you to create a specific element.

| Button | Shortcut keys |
| --- | --- |
|  | A |
|  | Shift + N |
|  | H |
|  | C |
|  | P |
|  | Shift + U |
|  | K |
|  | S |
|  | U |
|  | G |
|  | Shift + G |
|  | D |
|  | Shift + D |

If you use either **Inverse Object Properties** or **Unidirectional Object Property**, the following items will be created:

- When a property’s type does not have a name, “*unnamed property*” will be used as the property’s name.
- When a property’s type has a name, the name will be written in lower-case letters and recognized with “*has*"**(with a space after). For example, if the property’s type name is “*Boss Deck*”, it will be converted to “*has boss deck*”.

64854116

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Experimental Features' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a concept model' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ad7f38f-532f-4c53-aec4-a2ae8266c72a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64854118</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b1e775b-9dbb-4e5c-b088-de764deb3d96"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64854120</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a9f486-0dbc-49e2-a19b-c6a87362ee6b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64854119</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a concept modeling project</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>New Project</strong>. The <strong>New Project</strong> dialog will open.</li><li>Select <strong>Concept Modeling Project</strong>.</li><li>Name your project and select your <strong>Project location</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><p>A new Concept Modeling diagram will open, complete with the Concept Modeling diagram palette. This diagram and its palette will also open whenever you create a new Concept Modeling diagram.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DiagramPallete.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>The Concept Modeling diagram.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db63bfb-a7d8-45e3-8dd5-9273a0b84f8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Containment Tree</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display or hide concept modeler profiles as auxiliary resources in the Containment tree by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Drop-down List.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and selecting <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying Auxiliary Resources.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Setting concept modeler profiles as auxiliary resources to be displayed in the Containment tree.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Auxiliary Resources.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Concept modeler auxiliary resources are displayed in grey in the Containment tree.</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b3e815f-f1ee-45e0-9093-84703b21fa48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Subproperties</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="status" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b18c9ba-7044-401b-91ff-8f1ad24ada3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="colour">Green</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Available From 19.0 SP1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>You can view subproperties in the <strong>Subproperties Tree.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="subproperties.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can adjust the visibility of your subproperties using the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Drop-down List.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button as shown below. By default, <strong>Show Applied Stereotypes</strong> is selected. The figure below shows what happens when you set each option to <strong>True</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Alloptions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>Simple Properties are properties that do <strong>not</strong> have any subproperties attached to them. So, if you would like to see a simple property, do <strong>not</strong> select <strong>Hide Simple Properties.</strong></li></ul><p>You can also toggle the subproperties tree using the diagram pane. Right-click on any property in your diagram pane and select <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> &gt; <strong>Select in</strong> <strong>Subproperties Tree.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagp.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can also toggle the subproperties tree using the <strong>Main Menu.</strong> Select <strong>Window</strong> &gt; <strong>Subproperties.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="300"><ri:attachment ri:filename="s1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d868f6d8-b6b8-4f98-a0e3-9da3570b4c7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut Keys</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The following table shows the buttons in the Concept Modeling diagram palette which represent the elements you can use to create a Concept Modeling diagram. You can drag the button to a diagram to create that type of element. The shortcut key may also make it easier for you to create a specific element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Shortcut keys</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-29 21:41:8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>A</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-29 21:41:24.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Shift + N</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:54:7.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>H</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:54:40.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>C</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:54:54.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>P</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:55:2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Shift + U</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:55:12.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>K</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:55:23.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>S</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:55:37.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>U</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:55:48.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>G</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:56:0.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Shift + G</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:56:10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>D</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 9:56:19.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept modeling project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Shift + D</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="western">If you use either <strong>Inverse Object Properties</strong> or <strong>Unidirectional Object Property</strong>, the following items will be created: </p><ul><li><p>When a property’s type does not have a name, “<em>unnamed property</em>” will be used as the property’s name.</p></li><li><p>When a property’s type has a name, the name will be written in lower-case letters and recognized with “<em>has</em>&quot;<em> </em>(with a space after). For example, if the property’s type name is “<em>Boss Deck</em>”, it will be converted to “<em>has boss deck</em>”.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31870b28-1659-4249-b8cf-c5be76f86e33"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64854116</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usage" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Experimental Features" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept model" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478999 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: Creating a datatype property

- page_id: `170478999`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478999/Creating+a+datatype+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:20.834+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

GreenAvailable From 18.0 SP13

To model a datatype property as a class attribute

1. Create a class, name it Class 1 .
2. Click on the black circle with the white plus sign in the center of it. [ATTACHMENT filename='CE.png']
3. Select Property. [ATTACHMENT filename='propp.png']
4. A default property appears in the class. The property is also called a class attribute. [ATTACHMENT filename='unnamedproperty.png']
5. Double - click on the default property. Its Specification window appears. [ATTACHMENT filename='Type.png']
6. Click on the Type field. A shortcut menu appears. Select boolean. (or any datatype property you want)
7. Click Close.

The result should look like this:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The class element with a datatype property *unnamed1.***

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="status" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2052333f-5530-4e49-ad31-56598046e003"><ac:parameter ac:name="colour">Green</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Available From 18.0 SP13</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To model a datatype property as a class attribute</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a class, name it <em>Class </em><em>1</em>.</li><li>Click on the black circle with the white plus sign in the center of it.<ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="146"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CE.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a datatype property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Property.</strong><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="98"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propp.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a datatype property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>A default property appears in the class. The property is also called a <em>class attribute.</em><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="81"><ri:attachment ri:filename="unnamedproperty.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a datatype property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Double - click on the default property. Its <strong>Specification window</strong> appears.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Type.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a datatype property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click on the <strong>Type</strong> field. A shortcut menu appears. Select <em>boolean.</em> (or any datatype property you want)</li><li>Click <strong>Close.</strong></li></ol><p>The result should look like this:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="60"><ri:attachment ri:filename="datatypepropertyapplied.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a datatype property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>The class element with a datatype property <em>unnamed1.</em></strong></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479073 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: Creating a glossary table

- page_id: `170479073`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479073/Creating+a+glossary+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:23.539+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with glossaries
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1301832871

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1301832874

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1301832872

In a concept model, a glossary table contains the names and descriptions of classes, association ends, attributes, enumerations, and enumeration literals that are defined in the concept model.

To create a glossary table

1. Right-click on the owning package. NoteThe owning package must have the correct «Model» or «Concept Model» stereotype for these menus to appear.
2. Select **Concept Modeling**>**Create Glossary Table**(see the figure below). NoteIf a glossary table already exists in the owning package, the**Create Glossary Table**menu option will not be available.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Concept Modeler's Create Glossary Table menu.

At least one glossary generation project option must be enabled. If all of the options are turned off, the **Create Glossary Table** menu option will be disabled.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The glossary creation menu is disabled.

1301832869

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478771 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00084: Creating a path variable to share OWL import catalog files

- page_id: `170478771`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478771/Creating+a+path+variable+to+share+OWL+import+catalog+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:55.996+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

240438636

240438639

240438638

You are able to set your path variable to a local directory containing your OWL catalog files. In doing so, the modeling tool allows you to share OWL import catalogs and projects that use the same path variable. Without this path variable, you and your collaborative team may have different path variables to the OWL import catalog or project. This difference may cause you and your team to change the path variable back and forth. To resolve this issue, you need to define a path variable to your local directory that corresponds to the directory containing the same OWL import catalog on your team’s local directory.

To create a path variable

1. Click **Options** > **Environment**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Select Path Variables.
3. Click Add. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Name the path in the Name field.
5. Click the “…” button next to the Value text box.
6. Select desired root directory containing the OWL import catalog files . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 11:34:10.png']
7. Click **Open**.
8. Click **OK**. You will see the created directory appear on the Path Variables list. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click OK .

240464281

**Related pages**

- Usage
- Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting the OWL import catalog in MagicDraw' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using a path variable to share OWL import catalog files' space='MCM2024xR1']

In doing so, the modeling tool allows you to share OWL import catalogs and projects that use the same path variable. Without this path variable, you and your collaborative team may have different path variables to the OWL import catalog or project. This difference may cause you and your team to change the path variable back and forth. To resolve this issue, you need to define a path variable to your local directory that corresponds to the directory containing the same OWL import catalog on your team’s local directory.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a path variable</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="464"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 11:36:35.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a path variable to share OWL import catalog files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Select <strong>Path Variables.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Add.</strong><br /><strong><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="464"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 11:35:25.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a path variable to share OWL import catalog files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></li><li>Name the path in the <strong>Name</strong> field.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Click the “…” button next to the </span><strong>Value</strong><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"> text box.<br /></span></li><li>Select desired root directory <u>containing the OWL import catalog files</u>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="464"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 11:34:10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a path variable to share OWL import catalog files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Click <strong>Open</strong>.</p></li><li><p style="text-align: left;">Click <strong>OK</strong>. You will see the created directory appear on the Path Variables list.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 11:34:46.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a path variable to share OWL import catalog files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7cd1042-7942-426a-b732-11578eaee5d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">240464281</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Importing+an+OWL+ontology+to+a+concept+model">Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting the OWL import catalog in MagicDraw" /></ac:link></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using a path variable to share OWL import catalog files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478932 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: Creating a property chain

- page_id: `170478932`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478932/Creating+a+property+chain
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:18.312+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

65756480

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

65756482

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

65756481

The Concept Modeler allows you to create a property chain by dragging properties, one after another, to a target property. As explained [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property chain' space='MCM2024xR1'], the property chain allows you to navigate from a starting class (the one with the stereotype «Subproperty Chain») through a chain of properties that take a path through multiple classes.

The drag-and-drop actions of the properties provide the two following menu options:

- Create subproperty chain, and
- Add property to subproperty chain.

To create a property chain

1. Drag a property to be composed in the chain, e.g., *has father*, to a target property, e.g., *has uncle*, on the diagram pane. A shortcut menu opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] TipAlternatively, you can create a property chain by right-clicking a target property and selecting **Create subproperty chain** from the shortcut menu. You can then select the properties to be composed in the chain from the tree in the **Select Property** dialog.
2. Select **Create subproperty chain** from the shortcut menu. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Drag the next property to be composed in the chain, e.g., *has brother*, to the target property, e.g., *has uncle*, and select **Add property to subproperty chain**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The property chain will be created. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-29 22:36:58.png']

You can double-click the property “*has uncle*” to open its Specification window and see the tagged value of the property.

65756479

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deleting a property chain' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing a property chain' space='MCM2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property chain' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']

## PAGE 00086: Creating a property holder

- page_id: `170478923`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478923/Creating+a+property+holder
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:58.027+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: ['property-holder']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

316989400

316989403

316989401

To create a property holder (UML class stereotyped as «Anything»)

1. Create a UML class (named Thing ). [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 13:59:41.png'] A UML class.
2. Right-click the UML class and select Stereotype . [ATTACHMENT filename='class_shortcut_menu.png'] Selecting the class' Stereotype shortcut menu.
3. Type Anything in the search box.
4. Select the Anything stereotype.
5. Click Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting Anything Stereotype.png'] Selecting the Anything stereotype for the class. The «Anything» stereotype will be applied to the class. [ATTACHMENT filename='Anything Stereotype.png'] The «Anything» stereotype applied to the UML class.

316989399

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478751 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: Creating an XML catalog file

- page_id: `170478751`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478751/Creating+an+XML+catalog+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:13.189+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

236599862

236599864

236599863

An XML catalog describes the mapping between external entity references and locally cached equivalents for an XML external resource.

You can create an XML catalog file using the application **Protégé[**[1]**](file:///D:/doc/CCM%2018.0%20SP7/Concept%20Modeler%20Documentation.docx#_ftn1)**. The version of **Protégé** used in these instructions is version 4.3.0.

To create an XML catalog file for a locally cached set of external ontologies

1. Open Protégé .
2. Select**File**>**New.** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating an OWL ontology in Protégé application.
3. Select **Save as.** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Saving an OWL ontology in Protégé.
4. Navigate to a folder location.
5. Name the empty ontology.
6. Click **Save**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Saving the OWL ontology file to a selected location.
7. Click **File** > **Open**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Opening the OWL ontology in Protégé.
8. Click **Yes**. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:50:29.png'] Opening the OWL ontology in the current window option.
9. Select the newly created ontology.
10. Click Open . [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting an OWL ontology to open.

To create an XML catalog file for the desired locally cached set of external ontologies:

1. Click **File** > **Edit active ontology library**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating an XML catalog file in Protégé.
2. Click on the Folder Repository for the empty ontology.
3. Click Delete (-). [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Deleting a folder repository.
4. Click Add (+). [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Adding a folder repository.
5. Click on Folder Repository.
6. Select **Recursively search****subdirectories**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Locating for a folder repository.
7. Select the desired folder.
8. Click Open. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 11:19:7.png'] Selecting a folder repository.
9. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:57:35.png'] The new folder repository is added.

[[1]](file:///D:/doc/CCM%2018.0%20SP7/Concept%20Modeler%20Documentation.docx#_ftnref1) **Protégé**is a free, open-source ontology editor, and a framework for building knowledge management systems.

236599861

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479005 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00088: Creating and removing a necessary and sufficient condition

- page_id: `170479005`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479005/Creating+and+removing+a+necessary+and+sufficient+condition
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:58.952+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Support for an Aristotelian subclass definition

428160836

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Support for an Aristotelian subclass definition

428160838

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Support for an Aristotelian subclass definition

428160837

In the Concept Modeling interpretation of UML, a property that has the **Necessary and Sufficient** stereotype applied to it indicates that, when an instance satisfies the multiplicity and type constraints for the property’s values, not only is it a *necessary* condition to be an instance of the class met, but a *sufficient* condition is also met (see section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Conditions' space='CCMP']Condition).

#### NOTE: Support for an Aristotelian subclass definition

Support for an Aristotelian subclass definition

Making a generalization {sufficient} provides for correct support for an Aristotelian subclass definition, where the subclass is equivalent to the intersection of all of its sufficient conditions.

To create a sufficient condition

1. Right-click on the association end of the property to which the sufficient constraint will be applied (unnamed from *“Dog* *Owner*”). Remember that the property is owned by the class at the opposite end of the association.
2. Select **Concept Modeling** > **Add property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance**in the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Toggling the Add property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance shortcut menu.**

The **Necessary and Sufficient** stereotype is toggled on for the property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The Necessary and Sufficient stereotype is created.**

To remove a sufficient condition on a property

1. Right-click on the association end of the property of which the {sufficient} constraint will be removed (unnamed from *“Dog Owner”).*
2. Select **Concept Modeling > Remove**property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance******.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Toggling the Remove property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance shortcut menu.**

428160835

**Related page**

- Usage

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0789e85-fbef-4072-a835-ed5a0a6b9921"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">428160836</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dc8f56e-ca7d-4566-99dd-ff639bcae099"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">428160838</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eeab6540-72b4-41b4-9b53-a097c2ab6bef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">428160837</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Concept Modeling interpretation of UML, a property that has the <strong>Necessary and Sufficient</strong> stereotype applied to it indicates that, when an instance satisfies the multiplicity and type constraints for the property’s values, not only is it a <em>necessary</em> condition to be an instance of the class met, but a <em>sufficient</em> condition is also met (see section <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CCMP" ri:content-title="Conditions" /><ac:link-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Condition</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d2eb95e-3787-4957-868c-04773324fd56"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Support for an Aristotelian subclass definition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Making a generalization {sufficient} provides for correct support for an Aristotelian subclass definition, where the subclass is equivalent to the intersection of all of its sufficient conditions.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a sufficient condition</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click on the association end of the property to which the sufficient constraint will be applied (unnamed from <em>“Dog</em> <em>Owner</em>”). Remember that the property is owned by the class at the opposite end of the association.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> &gt; <strong>Add property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance </strong>in the shortcut menu.</span></p></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addproperty.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and removing a necessary and sufficient condition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Toggling the Add property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance shortcut menu.</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Necessary and Sufficient</strong> stereotype is toggled on for the property.</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="144"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NS.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and removing a necessary and sufficient condition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>The Necessary and Sufficient stereotype is created.</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To remove a sufficient condition on a property</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click on the association end of the property of which the {sufficient} constraint will be removed (unnamed from <em>“Dog Owner”).</em></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select <strong>Concept Modeling &gt; Remove<strong> property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance</strong></strong><strong>.</strong></span></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NSRemove.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and removing a necessary and sufficient condition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Toggling the Remove property constraints to the conditions sufficient to classify an instance shortcut menu.</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="718e3c2b-28ad-4b6f-9cbb-ae8ce492b4f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">428160835</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage" rel="nofollow">Usage</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479087 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00089: Creating annotations

- page_id: `170479087`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479087/Creating+annotations

- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:24.577+02:00`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: IRI Annotation

1048778085

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: IRI Annotation

1048778087

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: IRI Annotation

1048778086

#### INFO: IRI Annotation

IRI Annotation

Creating an IRI Annotation is done the same way as creating a Literal Annotation

To create a Literal Annotation and anchoring it to a class

1. In the concept modeling project, click the Concept Modeling palette menu to expand it. Select Literal Annotation . [ATTACHMENT filename='Annotation Menu.png']
2. Hold the mouse button to draw the Annotation. The Literal Annotation will be created. [ATTACHMENT filename='Literal Annotation.png']
3. In the Concept Modeling palette menu, select Anchor .
4. Hold the mouse button from the created Literal Annotation and draw the Anchor to any element, e.g., Class, to anchor it. [ATTACHMENT filename='Drawing Anchor to Literal Annotation.png']
5. The Literal Annotation will be anchored with the chosen element. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

1277853994

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with annotations' space='MCM2024xR1']

Select <strong>Literal Annotation</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Annotation Menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating annotations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Hold the mouse button to draw the Annotation. ## PAGE 00090: Creating equivalent classes

- page_id: `170478889`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478889/Creating+equivalent+classes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:16.816+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

65638028

65638030

65638029

To create equivalent classes

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the Concept Modeling diagram palette.
2. In the diagram pane, click on a class and drag the line to another class to make them equivalent to each other. A double-headed arrow will be created between the two classes, and the stereotype**«****Equivalent Class****»**will be visible. [IMAGE alt='' src='']Creating class equivalence between two classes. 
 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']The classes "US President" and "Principal Resident of White House" are equivalent to each other.

65656357

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478958 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00091: Creating equivalent properties

- page_id: `170478958`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478958/Creating+equivalent+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:58.289+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

65549713

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

65549715

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

65549714

Properties can be equivalent to each other. You can make two or more properties equivalent by dragging a property to the target property. The Concept Modeler provides you with the following shortcut menus:

- Create equivalent property.
- Add property to equivalent property.

A property is semantically equivalent to another property if it is stereotyped with**«Equivalent Property»,**and its tagged value is **equivalent to**.

To create two or more equivalent properties

1. Drag a property, e.g., “ has dad ” to a target property, e.g., “ has father ” in the diagram pane. The shortcut menu will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:19:11.png'] The Create equivalent properties shortcut menu.
2. Select Create equivalent property . The property “ has father ” is now equivalent to the property “ has dad ”. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:20:18.png'] Two equivalent properties "*has father*" and "*has dad*".
3. You may add more properties to the existing equivalent properties by dragging the next property, e.g., “*has papa”* to the equivalent property “*has father*” in the diagram pane. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Add equivalent property shortcut menu.
4. Select Add property to equivalent property . The property “ has papa ” is now equivalent to the properties “ has father ” and “ has dad ”. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:23:26.png'] Equivalent properties in Concept Modeler. TipAlternatively, you can make a property equivalent to one or more properties by right-clicking on it and selecting **Concept Modeling** > **Create equivalent property** from the shortcut menu, and select one or more properties from the tree in the **Select Property** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']The Select Property dialog.

65954172

**Related pages**

- Usage
- Creating a concept model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3b057ea-c7e0-4ca1-82d2-6325c82fbceb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65549713</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4ecf61c-42c6-4639-a230-6b1ca557a2de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65549715</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf7f73e1-3c90-4f6f-9daa-120c323faf7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65549714</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Properties can be equivalent to each other. You can make two or more properties equivalent by dragging a property to the target property. The Concept Modeler provides you with the following shortcut menus:</p><ul><li>Create equivalent property.</li><li>Add property to equivalent property.</li></ul><p>A property is semantically equivalent to another property if it is stereotyped with<u> </u><strong>«Equivalent Property», </strong>and its tagged value is <strong>equivalent to</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create two or more equivalent properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag a property, e.g., “<em>has dad</em>” to a target property, e.g., “<em>has father</em>” in the diagram pane. The shortcut menu will open.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 10:19:11.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Create equivalent properties shortcut menu.</h6></li><li>Select <strong>Create equivalent property</strong>. The property “<em>has father</em>” is now equivalent to the property “<em>has dad</em>”.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 10:20:18.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Two equivalent properties &quot;<em>has father</em>&quot; and &quot;<em>has dad</em>&quot;.</h6></li><li><p>You may add more properties to the existing equivalent properties by dragging the next property, e.g., “<em>has papa”</em> to the equivalent property “<em>has father</em>” in the diagram pane.</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 10:21:31.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Add equivalent property shortcut menu.</h6></li><li>Select <strong>Add property to equivalent property</strong>. The property “<em>has papa</em>” is now equivalent to the properties “<em>has father</em>” and “<em>has dad</em>”.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 10:23:26.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Equivalent properties in Concept Modeler.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6348e7a-e65d-4644-b3bd-e4384fe68cd4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Alternatively, you can make a property equivalent to one or more properties by right-clicking on it and selecting <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> &gt; <strong>Create equivalent property</strong> from the shortcut menu, and select one or more properties from the tree in the <strong>Select Property</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 10:24:55.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Select Property dialog.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46064f32-7fea-4a00-b72e-86137ad4a4b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65954172</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Creating+a+concept+model">Creating a concept model</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479028 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: Creating Restrictions

- page_id: `170479028`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479028/Creating+Restrictions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:21.874+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with restrictions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

323242002

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

323242004

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

323242003

To create a Restriction using **Property Restriction** on the **Concept Modeling** diagram palette or on the smart manipulator toolbar

1. Do one of the following
  - On the Concept Modeling diagram palette, select Property Restriction as shown in the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='Property Restriction Button.png']
  - Select an element in the diagram, (e.g., Class). When the smart manipulator toolbar appears, select the [ATTACHMENT filename='Property Restriction Button on Smart Manipulator.png'] Property Restriction button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Smart Manipulator for Restriction.png']
2. Point to an element, (e.g., Dog Lover Class), and select it while dragging the arrow line to another element on which you want to create the Restriction. [ATTACHMENT filename='First Creating Property Restriction.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Property Restriction.png']
3. The Edit the restriction dialog will open automatically to configure and create the Restriction. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing Restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']Edit the settings in the **Edit the restriction** dialog, starting from Step 2 . [ATTACHMENT filename='Restriction Created with Dialog.png']
4. Click OK . The Restriction will be created.

To create a universal quantification constraint

1. Drag and drop a property to be redefined (e.g., *has* from *Person*) onto a redefining property (e.g., *has* from *Dog Lover*). NoteThe property is owned by the class at the opposite end of the association.The target can have the same name as the source, or can be unnamed.The newly redefined property's multiplicity is adjusted to conform to the multiplicity of the dragged, redefined property. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Dragging the property to be redefined to the redefining property.
2. Select Specify that objects may only be from type '{}' . [ATTACHMENT filename='Universal Constraint Context Menu.png'] The "Specify that objects may only be from type 'Dog'" shortcut menu.
3. The universal quantification constraint will be created. [ATTACHMENT filename='Result of Universal Constraint.png'] The created, redefined universal quantification constraint.

To create an existential quantification constraint

1. Drag and drop a property you want to subset (e.g., *has* from *Person*) onto the cardinality of the property that will subset another property (e.g., *unnamed* from *Dog Caretaker*). NoteThe target can have the same name as the source, or can be unnamed.The resulting subsetting property’s multiplicity is adjusted to conform to the dragged, subsetted property, and to have a minimum cardinality of at least one. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Dragging the property onto a cardinality of another property to create an existential quantification constraint.
2. Do one of the following [ATTACHMENT filename='Existential Context Menu.png'] The "Specify multiplicity for type 'Dog'" and "Specify that some objects must be from type 'Dog'" commands.
  - Select Specify multiplicity for type '{}' to specify the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more.
  - Select Specify that some objects must be from type '{}' to specify the lower value of multiplicity of 1.
3. The existential quantification constraint will be created. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The created existential quantification constraint as subset with the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more when selecting the "Specify multiplicity for type 'Dog'" command. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The created existential quantification constraint as subset with the lower value of multiplicity of 1 when selecting the "Specify that some objects must be from type 'Dog'" command.

323250482

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing Restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a90bcb9f-20de-4268-a1e0-b08383e6ad69"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">323242002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5247c264-c800-428c-867c-5d11b348a809"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">323242004</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d1f1ebb-66e9-4b6f-814a-5f3c8c89c462"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">323242003</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a Restriction using <strong>Property Restriction</strong> on the <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> diagram palette or on the smart manipulator toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following<br /><br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> diagram palette, select <strong>Property Restriction</strong> as shown in the figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Restriction Button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select an element in the diagram, (e.g., Class). When the smart manipulator toolbar appears, select the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Restriction Button on Smart Manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Property Restriction</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Smart Manipulator for Restriction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Point to an element, (e.g., <em>Dog Lover</em> Class), and select it while dragging the arrow line to another element on which you want to create the Restriction.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="First Creating Property Restriction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating Property Restriction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The <strong>Edit the restriction</strong> dialog will open automatically to configure and create the Restriction.  <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /><ac:link-body>Edit the settings in the <strong>Edit the restriction</strong> dialog, starting from Step 2</ac:link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Restriction Created with Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The Restriction will be created.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a universal quantification constraint</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Drag and drop a property to be redefined (e.g., <em>has</em> from <em>Person</em>) onto a redefining property (e.g., <em>has</em> from <em>Dog Lover</em>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c74d4a8-baf3-4bea-b886-99c3b63425f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The property is owned by the class at the opposite end of the association.</li><li>The target can have the same name as the source, or can be unnamed.</li><li>The newly redefined property's multiplicity is adjusted to conform to the multiplicity of the dragged, redefined property.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Universal Constraint.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dragging the property to be redefined to the redefining property.</h6></li><li>Select <strong>Specify that objects may only be from type '{}'</strong>.<br /><br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Universal Constraint Context Menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The &quot;Specify that objects may only be from type 'Dog'&quot; shortcut menu.</h6></li><li><p>The universal quantification constraint will be created.</p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Result of Universal Constraint.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The created, redefined universal quantification constraint.</h6></li></ol><p>To create an existential quantification constraint</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Drag and drop a property you want to subset (e.g., <em>has</em> from <em>Person</em>) onto the cardinality of the property that will subset another property (e.g., <em>unnamed</em> from <em>Dog Caretaker</em>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fcc16c8-ade4-492e-b6a8-b62ef366c853"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The target can have the same name as the source, or can be unnamed.</li><li>The resulting subsetting property’s multiplicity is adjusted to conform to the dragged, subsetted property, and to have a minimum cardinality of at least one.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Building Existential Constraint.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dragging the property onto a cardinality of another property to create an existential quantification constraint.</h6></li><li>Do one of the following<br /><ul><li>Select <strong>Specify multiplicity for type '{}'</strong> to specify the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more.</li><li>Select <strong>Specify that some objects must be from type '{}' </strong>to specify the lower value of multiplicity of 1.</li></ul><br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Existential Context Menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The &quot;Specify multiplicity for type 'Dog'&quot; and &quot;Specify that some objects must be from type 'Dog'&quot; commands.<br /><br /></h6></li><li><p>The existential quantification constraint will be created.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Result of Existential Constraint 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The created existential quantification constraint as subset with the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more when selecting the &quot;Specify multiplicity for type 'Dog'&quot; command.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Result of Existential Constraint.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The created existential quantification constraint as subset with the lower value of multiplicity of 1 when selecting the &quot;Specify that some objects must be from type 'Dog'&quot; command.</h6></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3b10e9e-36f8-499e-b9c2-471917d49e49"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">323250482</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usage" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with restrictions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Removing Restrictions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478599 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00093: Customizing AutoStyler

- page_id: `170478599`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478599/Customizing+AutoStyler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:07.429+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > AutoStyler Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

One of the features of AutoStyler is customization. All default features of AutoStyler can be customized thus making the plugin more flexible and compatible with your needs. This page will include several detailed examples of customizing AutoStyler; however, this page itself will serve as an introduction. More information about customization are found at the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How to change the color of AutoStyler' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How to remove package names of classes' space='MCM2024xR1']

To access the customization page for AutoStyler

1. Click on Options > Project .
2. Click on Defined Elsewhere . Here you can customize AutoStyler .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Defined Elsewhere Page

To automatically apply customizations

1. Once you have adjusted your customizations, find the word**Apply**and click on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Make sure to select the correct diagram you would like to apply your customizations to.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. After you select your diagram and click **OK** you will be led to another page where you will also click **OK**. You have now automatically applied all the customizations you wanted to make to all the classes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>One of the features of AutoStyler is customization. All default features of AutoStyler can be customized thus making the plugin more flexible and compatible with your needs. This page will include several detailed examples of customizing AutoStyler; however, this page itself will serve as an introduction. More information about customization are found at the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="How to change the color of AutoStyler" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="How to remove package names of classes" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To access the customization page for AutoStyler</p><hr /><ol><li>Click on <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>Click on<strong> Defined Elsewhere</strong>. Here you can customize <strong>AutoStyler</strong>.</li></ol><p>     <ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250" ac:width="393"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DefinedElsewhere.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing AutoStyler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: center;">Defined Elsewhere Page</h4></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>To automatically apply customizations</p><hr /><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">        1. Once you have adjusted your customizations, find the word </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Apply</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and click on it.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="128" ac:width="498"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ApplyButton.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing AutoStyler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>        2. Make sure to select the correct diagram you would like to apply your customizations to.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="400" ac:width="311"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BlackBoxesChoosingFromDiagrams.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing AutoStyler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">        3. After you select your diagram and click <strong>OK</strong> you will be led to another page where you will also click <strong>OK</strong>. You have now automatically applied all the customizations you wanted to make to all the classes.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00095: Defining an annotation property

- page_id: `170479128`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479128/Defining+an+annotation+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:26.578+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with annotations > Working with annotation properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternate way using the diagram pane

1048755075

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternate way using the diagram pane

1048755078

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternate way using the diagram pane

1048755077

umlpropTo create a UML Property

1. In the Containment Tree, right-click on your desired element inside your concept model.
2. Select Create Element.
3. A menu appears like shown in the following figure. Click on Property. [ATTACHMENT filename='creatingprop.png']
4. Name your newly created Property and see the result on the diagram pane.

#### TIP: Alternate Way

Alternate Way

Property

Association

Classes,

Class

Concept Modeler has annotation property defined as a stereotype. You can always add Annotation Properties manually in your Concept Modeling project. The following instructions show you how to create an Annotation Property in your model.

To apply an Annotation Property stereotype for a property

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining an annotation property' space='MCM2024xR1'] as described above.
2. Double - click on the property to open its specification window.
3. In the specification window, click the three dots at the end of the Applied Stereotype field and select Annotation Property .
4. Click Apply .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### TIP: Alternate way using the diagram pane

Alternate way using the diagram pane

Another way to apply the annotation property stereotype to your property is to select the property in the **diagram pane** and **right** **- click** so that a shortcut menu appears. Click on **Stereotype**, and you will see the same **Stereotype** dialog pop up as the one shown on the **right** in the figure above.

1277865169

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3adae5b0-c620-4e08-b51d-13d4e2da1e68"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048755075</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eaf8fe75-05fe-43bc-997d-558af189417a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048755078</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e99f9370-90f1-41a2-8355-c030aedcade2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048755077</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a66ee73b-e37d-4ded-86e0-ed63acf09665"><ac:parameter ac:name="">umlprop</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a UML Property</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, <strong>right-click</strong> on your desired <em>element</em> inside your concept model.</li><li>Select <strong>Create Element.</strong></li><li>A menu appears like shown in the following figure. Click on <strong>Property.</strong><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creatingprop.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining an annotation property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Name your newly created <strong>Property</strong> and see the result on the diagram pane. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60108b57-c7fa-4035-875f-72256dc2a760"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternate Way</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Another way to create a <strong>Property</strong> is to create an <strong>Association</strong> between two <em>Classes,</em> or between a <em>Class</em> and itself.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Concept Modeler has annotation property defined as a stereotype. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can always add Annotation Properties manually in your Concept Modeling project. The following instructions show you how to create an Annotation Property in your model.</span></p><p>To apply an Annotation Property stereotype for a property</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create a <ac:link ac:anchor="umlprop"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining an annotation property" /></ac:link> as described above.</p></li><li><strong>Double - click</strong> on the property to open its specification window.</li><li>In the specification window, click the <em>three dots</em> at the end of the <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong> field and select <strong>Annotation Property</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Applying Annotation Property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining an annotation property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="440fde06-c031-4a5d-871e-043e61e518ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternate way using the diagram pane</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Another way to apply the annotation property stereotype to your property is to select the property in the <strong>diagram pane</strong> and <strong>right</strong> <strong>- click</strong> so that a shortcut menu appears. Click on <strong>Stereotype</strong>, and you will see the same <strong>Stereotype</strong> dialog pop up as the one shown on the <strong>right</strong> in the figure above.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9ea67c6-0f3c-46c6-a4ee-bb5c39a85720"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277865169</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usage" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478953 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: Deleting a property chain

- page_id: `170478953`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478953/Deleting+a+property+chain
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:18.897+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternate Way

67367368

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternate Way

67367370

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternate Way

67367369

You can delete a property chain using the shortcut menu: **Remove subproperty chain**.

To delete a property chain

1. Right-click a target property, e.g., *has uncle*, or a property chain, e.g., *{chain = ...}* in the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Using the Remove subproperty chain shortcut menu to delete a property chain.
2. Select **Concept Modeling** > **Remove subproperty chain** for the shortcut menu. The Concept Modeler will delete all of the properties in the selected property chain. Alternate Way1. Select **Concept Modeling** > **Edit subproperty chain**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']2. Click on the Property Chain, then click **Remove Value.**[IMAGE alt='' src='']3. Click **Close.**

67375803

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a concept model' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a property chain' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cd1c30e-4db0-41e2-9250-d75cd03b99ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">67367368</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a24f464-cd30-466f-a334-0d4a0d6b51ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">67367370</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2bfe517-cdae-4539-9ddb-22a1dd42ebca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">67367369</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="western">You can delete a property chain using the shortcut menu: <strong>Remove subproperty chain</strong>.</p><p class="western"><br /></p><p class="western">To delete a property chain</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Right-click a target property, e.g., <em>has uncle</em>, or a property chain, e.g., <em>{chain = ...}</em> in the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-29 22:44:58.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deleting a property chain" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using the Remove subproperty chain shortcut menu to delete a property chain.</h6></li><li><p>Select <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove subproperty chain</strong> for the shortcut menu. The Concept Modeler will delete all of the properties in the selected property chain.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5876e57-97a5-4db7-b2eb-408e3de240ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternate Way</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>1. Select <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Concept Modeling</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Edit subproperty chain</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Alternateway.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deleting a property chain" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>2. Click on the Property Chain, then click <strong>Remove Value.</strong></p><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="RemovingValue.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Deleting a property chain" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p><br /></p><p>3. Click <strong>Close.</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="faa992b1-16ca-442c-be44-b121e1dd2363"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">67375803</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usage" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a concept model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating a property chain" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00097: Deleting an equivalent property

- page_id: `170478972`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478972/Deleting+an+equivalent+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:19.594+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

69371588

69371590

69371589

To delete an equivalent property

1. Right-click the target property, e.g. “ has father {equivalent to = has dad, has papa} ”, in the diagram pane. The shortcut menu will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:26:55.png'] The Remove equivalent properties shortcut menu.
2. Select Concept Modeling > Remove equivalent properties . The Concept Modeler will remove all of the equivalent properties.

69380182
