<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478597 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: Disable Auto Diagram Assignment

- page_id: `170478597`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478597/Disable+Auto+Diagram+Assignment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:07.313+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > AutoStyler Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Disable Auto Diagram Assignment** function is used to negate the functions of Enable Auto Diagram Assignment. The primary advantage of this function is that it allows you to reassign a class to a different defining diagram.

To use the **Disable Auto Diagram Assignment** function

1. Left-click anywhere on the diagram and select Disable Auto Diagram Assignment.

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Showing how to select the Disable Auto Diagram Assignment option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Disable Auto Diagram Assignment</strong> function is used to negate the functions of Enable Auto Diagram Assignment. The primary advantage of this function is that it allows you to reassign a class to a different defining diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use the <strong>Disable Auto Diagram Assignment</strong> function</p><hr /><ol><li>Left-click anywhere on the diagram and select <strong>Disable Auto Diagram Assignment.</strong></li></ol><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="293" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BetterQualityAutoStylerToDisableAutoDiagramAssignment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Disable Auto Diagram Assignment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: center;">Showing how to select the Disable Auto Diagram Assignment option.</h4><p><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478398 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00099: Disjoint classes

- page_id: `170478398`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478398/Disjoint+classes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:58.460+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > UML to Equivalent OWL in OWL Functional Syntax
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

5492802

5492804

5492803

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Disjoint dependency.

```text
Declaration(Class(:Daughter))
      Declaration(Class(:Parent))
      Declaration(Class(:Son))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:caredForBy))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:caresFor))
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Daughter "Daughter"@en)
      DisjointClasses(:Daughter :Son)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Parent "Parent"@en)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Son "Son"@en)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :caredForBy "cared for by"@en)
      InverseObjectProperties(:caredForBy :caresFor)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:caredForBy ObjectUnionOf(:Son :Daughter))
      ObjectPropertyRange(:caredForBy :Parent)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :caresFor "cares for"@en)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:caresFor :Parent)
      ObjectPropertyRange(:caresFor ObjectUnionOf(:Son :Daughter))
)]]>
```

5492801

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML to Equivalent OWL in OWL Functional Syntax' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1875da5f-e64d-40a7-b535-af8d02c8a3d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">5492802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1d330ef-7c21-4551-afce-71bf874adb02"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">5492804</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa1c2afe-d9b5-483e-a510-020efc986da4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">5492803</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-24 14:48:3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Disjoint classes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Disjoint dependency.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ca0b354-4566-440e-bff6-f4ec84bb2eb7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Ontology(<http://www.example.com/ontology/Disjoint>
      Declaration(Class(:Daughter))
      Declaration(Class(:Parent))
      Declaration(Class(:Son))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:caredForBy))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:caresFor))
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Daughter "Daughter"@en)
      DisjointClasses(:Daughter :Son)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Parent "Parent"@en)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Son "Son"@en)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :caredForBy "cared for by"@en)
      InverseObjectProperties(:caredForBy :caresFor)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:caredForBy ObjectUnionOf(:Son :Daughter))
      ObjectPropertyRange(:caredForBy :Parent)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :caresFor "cares for"@en)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:caresFor :Parent)
      ObjectPropertyRange(:caresFor ObjectUnionOf(:Son :Daughter))
)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61a91456-db5b-4376-8eef-8384d20539e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">5492801</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML to Equivalent OWL in OWL Functional Syntax" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478246 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00100: Disjoint subclasses

- page_id: `170478246`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478246/Disjoint+subclasses
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:54.155+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

74506875

74506878

74506877

This variation means that an instance can only be classified by one of the disjoint classes. Disjoint classes cannot have any overlap in their instances.

The following diagram shows three instances. One is an instance of “Cat”, one is an instance of “Dog”, and one is an instance of “Animal”. An instance classified as both “Cat” and “Dog” is impossible because there is no overlap between the two classes. In the most basic terms, an instance of a “Cat” cannot be an instance of a “Dog”, and vice versa.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Disjoint instances.

The diagram below shows an example of disjoint subclasses in standard UML notation. It shows that “Dog”, “Cat”, and “Mouse” are all subclasses of “Animal”. In addition, the standard UML {incomplete, disjoint} notation declares all of the subclasses to be incomplete and disjoint. Intuitively, an instance of the subclass “Dog” is an instance of the superclass “Animal”, but it cannot be an instance of the “Cat” or “Mouse” subclasses. Moreover, a lizard would be an instance of “Animal”, but could not be an instance of any of the subclasses “Dog”, “Cat”, or “Mouse”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Incomplete and disjoint subclasses in standard UML notation.

The Concept Modeler supports importing disjoint classes. A dependency stereotyped as «Disjoint With» will be used to specify disjoint subclasses. For example, the class Animal has two disjoint subclasses, Cat and Dog. When you import them to the Concept Modeler, the diagram will look similar to the example shown bellow.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Imported disjoint subclasses are stereotyped with «Disjoint With».

74506874

Disjoint classes cannot have any overlap in their instances.</p><p>The following diagram shows three instances. One is an instance of “Cat”, one is an instance of “Dog”, and one is an instance of “Animal”. An instance classified as both “Cat” and “Dog” is impossible because there is no overlap between the two classes. In the most basic terms, an instance of a “Cat” cannot be an instance of a “Dog”, and vice versa.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-23 11:58:28.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Disjoint subclasses" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Disjoint instances.</h6><p>The diagram below shows an example of disjoint subclasses in standard UML notation. It shows that “Dog”, “Cat”, and “Mouse” are all subclasses of “Animal”. In addition, the standard UML {incomplete, disjoint} notation declares all of the subclasses to be incomplete and disjoint. Intuitively, an instance of the subclass “Dog” is an instance of the superclass “Animal”, but it cannot be an instance of the “Cat” or “Mouse” subclasses. Moreover, a lizard would be an instance of “Animal”, but could not be an instance of any of the subclasses “Dog”, “Cat”, or “Mouse”.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-23 11:59:3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Disjoint subclasses" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Incomplete and disjoint subclasses in standard UML notation.</h6><p>The Concept Modeler supports importing disjoint classes. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478884 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00101: Displaying and hiding IRI

- page_id: `170478884`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478884/Displaying+and+hiding+IRI
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:57.510+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

241001604

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

241001607

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

241001606

The IRIs of classes and properties may not be visible in the diagram pane. You can display them by using the shortcut menu **Display IRI**. To hide the IRIs from the diagram pane, you can select the shortcut menu **Hide IRI**.

#### INFO: Newly create IRI tagged values

Newly create IRI tagged values

Newly created IRI tagged values are hidden in diagrams.

To display or hide the IRI tagged value of a class or an association end in the diagram pane

1. Right-click on a class or an association end in the diagram pane.
2. Select Concept Modeling, and select either Display IRI or Hide IRI.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**The Display IRI menu**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**The Hide IRI menu**

To display or hide the IRI tagged values of all classes or association ends in the diagram pane

1. On the main menu, click Edit > Select All . All of the elements in the diagram pane will be selected.
2. Right-click on any element.
3. Select Concept Modeling, and select either Display IRI or Hide IRI .

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The menu Display IRI will appear when you right-click on an element whose IRI is hidden from the diagram pane.
- The menu Hide IRI will appear when you right-click on an element whose IRI is displayed in the diagram pane.

241011639

**Related pages**

- Usage
- Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing annotations on an OWL ontology to a concept model' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

You can display them by using the shortcut menu <strong>Display IRI</strong>. To hide the IRIs from the diagram pane, you can select the shortcut menu <strong>Hide IRI</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a22eda2-34f3-46c9-abe7-e7542e80d3ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Newly create IRI tagged values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Newly created IRI tagged values are hidden in diagrams.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To display or hide the IRI tagged value of a class or an association end in the diagram pane</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click on a class or an association end in the diagram pane.</li><li>Select <strong>Concept Modeling,</strong> and select either <strong>Display IRI</strong> or <strong>Hide IRI.</strong><strong style="text-align: center;"><br /></strong></li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:width="464"><ri:attachment ri:filename="displayiri.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying and hiding IRI" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>The Display IRI menu</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:height="42"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hideiri.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Displaying and hiding IRI" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>The Hide IRI menu</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To display or hide the IRI tagged values of all classes or association ends in the diagram pane</span></p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Select All</strong>. All of the elements in the diagram pane will be selected.</li><li>Right-click on any element.</li><li>Select <strong>Concept Modeling,</strong> and select either <strong>Display IRI</strong> or <strong>Hide IRI</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a396d9c-bb75-45db-9180-6c85e7afbbc3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The menu <strong>Display IRI</strong> will appear when you right-click on an element whose IRI is hidden from the diagram pane.</li><li>The menu <strong>Hide IRI </strong>will appear when you right-click on an element whose IRI is displayed in the diagram pane. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e782ee5-2b50-4eb5-95d5-22c39bb5836a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241011639</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Importing+an+OWL+ontology+to+a+concept+model">Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Importing annotations on an OWL ontology to a concept model" /></ac:link></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478937 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00102: Editing a property chain

- page_id: `170478937`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478937/Editing+a+property+chain
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:58.138+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example changing the order of the property chain

67426164

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example changing the order of the property chain

67426166

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example changing the order of the property chain

67426165

You can use the shortcut menu option **Edit subproperty chain** to edit a property chain, and to add, remove, or reorder properties in a chain in the **Specification** window.

To edit a property chain

1. Right-click a property chain, e.g., *{chain = ...}* in the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Edit subproperty chain shortcut menu to edit a property chain.
2. Select **Concept Modeling** > **Edit subproperty chain**. The **Specification of Property has uncle** dialog of the property will open showing the property chain, e.g., *{chain = ...}* in the **Tags** section. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Specification window of the property "*has uncle*".
3. Click the tagged value, e.g., chain = has father, has brother .
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:10:5.png'] . The Specification of Slot < > window will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:10:54.png'] Editing the property chain in the Specification window.
5. Click Value and click the properties box next to it.
6. You can click: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add a property to the property chain. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a property from the property chain. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to order the properties in the property chain.

#### EXPAND: Example adding a property to the chain

Example adding a property to the chain

1. Click on [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:12:42.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='AddPropertyChain.png']
2. The Select Element dialog opens. Type the name of the property you want to add to the property chain. Click OK. [ATTACHMENT filename='Findanelementtoadd.png']
3. The Specification of Element Value <> opens. Click Close. [ATTACHMENT filename='Closingspecificationwindowofthenewelement.png']

After clicking Close, you should see the new property added onto your property chain.

#### EXPAND: Example deleting a selected property from the chain

Example deleting a selected property from the chain

1. Click to highlight the property you would like to remove. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='minus_but.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png']
2. The property is removed from the property chain. [ATTACHMENT filename='removed.png']

#### EXPAND: Example changing the order of the property chain

Example changing the order of the property chain

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:12:59.png'] .
2. The Order Value dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch.png']
3. In the Order Value dialog, select the property you want and click either Up or Down accordingly. Click OK. [ATTACHMENT filename='switching.png']
4. Click Close. The properties in the property chain have been switched.

67498229

## PAGE 00103: Editing an equivalent property

- page_id: `170478964`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`

69752071

69752073

69752072

To edit an equivalent property

1. Right-click on an equivalent property, e.g. “ has father ”, in the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:30:7.png'] The Edit equivalent properties shortcut menu.
2. Select Concept Modeling > Edit equivalent properties . The Specification of Property has father window will open showing the equivalent properties under the section Tags . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:31:6.png'] The Specification window of Property has father.
3. Click the tagged value, e.g. equivalent to = has dad, has papa .
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:29:1.png'] . The Specification of Slot < > window will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:32:38.png'] The Specification window of Slot <>.
5. Click Value and click the properties box next to it.
6. You can click: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add another equivalent property. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected equivalent property. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to order the equivalent properties in the **Order Value** dialog.

69763352

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479051 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00104: Editing Restrictions

- page_id: `170479051`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479051/Editing+Restrictions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:59.176+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with restrictions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

664743435

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

664743446

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

664743445

To edit a Restriction

1. Right-click on a created property, and do one of the following
  - If the created property is not currently assigned with a Restriction, select Concept Modeling > Change to a property restriction only in the scope of class '{}' . [ATTACHMENT filename='Editing Restriction.png']
  - If the created property is currently assigned with a Restriction with the lower value of multiplicity of 0, select Concept Modeling > Change specification that objects may only be from type '{}' . [ATTACHMENT filename='Editing Restriction (0 or more).png']
  - If the created property is currently assigned with a Restriction with the lower value of multiplicity of 1, select Concept Modeling > Change specification that some objects must be from type '{}' . [ATTACHMENT filename='Editing Restriction (1 or more).png']
  - If the created property is currently assigned with a Restriction with the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more, select Concept Modeling > Change specification of multiplicity for type '{}' . [ATTACHMENT filename='Editing Restriction (2 or more).png']
2. The **Edit the restriction** dialog opens. At the **Restricted property** option, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the **Select the restricted property** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] NoteIf you apply the «Restriction» stereotype to a non-restricting property, the **Edit the restriction** dialog will open automatically. TipYou can also select the restricted property from the suggestion list by pressing **CTRL** + **SPACE** when selecting the **Restricted property** option.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Select the restricted property dialog, choose a restricted property and select OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Select the Restricted Property Dialog.png']
4. From the Select a restriction type list, select one of the following items [ATTACHMENT filename='Edit the Restriction Dialog 2.png']
  - Select Specify multiplicity for type '{}' for an existential quantification constraint with the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more.
  - Select Specify that some objects must be from type '{}' for an existential quantification constraint with the lower value of multiplicity of 1.
  - Select Specify that objects may only be from type '{}' for a universal quantification constraint.
5. In the **Min cardinality** and **Max cardinality**boxes, specify a lower value and higher value of multiplicity. NoteIf you want to create the self Restriction, select the **Is Self restriction** option.
6. Select OK . The Restriction will be edited according to the edited setting. [ATTACHMENT filename='Edited Restriction.png']

664743434

**Related pages**

- Usage
- Restriction
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']
- Removing Restrictions

/><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Editing Restriction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>If the created property is currently assigned with a Restriction with the lower value of multiplicity of 0, select <strong>Concept Modeling </strong>&gt;<strong> Change specification that objects may only be from type '{}'</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Editing Restriction (0 or more).png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>If the created property is currently assigned with a Restriction with the lower value of multiplicity of 1, select <strong>Concept Modeling </strong>&gt;<strong> Change specification that some objects must be from type '{}'</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Editing Restriction (1 or more).png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>If the created property is currently assigned with a Restriction with the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more, select <strong>Concept Modeling </strong>&gt;<strong> Change specification of multiplicity for type '{}'</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Editing Restriction (2 or more).png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Edit the restriction</strong> dialog opens. At the <strong>Restricted property</strong> option, select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the <strong>Select the restricted property</strong> dialog.<br /><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit the Restriction Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91571bdb-14c1-40ba-9d7f-1071636321cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you apply the «Restriction» stereotype to a non-restricting property, the <strong>Edit the restriction</strong> dialog will open automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bf9b7dd-9ecb-46ab-b0a0-5fbe4ba7c9bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select the restricted property from the suggestion list by pressing <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> when selecting the <strong>Restricted property</strong> option.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Restriction on Suggestion List.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the <strong>Select the restricted property</strong> dialog, choose a restricted property and select <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select the Restricted Property Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>From the <strong>Select a restriction type</strong> list, select one of the following items<br /><ul><li>Select <strong>Specify multiplicity for type '{}'</strong> for an existential quantification constraint with the lower value of multiplicity of 2 or more.</li><li>Select <strong>Specify that some objects must be from type '{}' </strong>for an existential quantification constraint with the lower value of multiplicity of 1.</li><li>Select <strong>Specify that objects may only be from type '{}'</strong> for a universal quantification constraint.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit the Restriction Dialog 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Min cardinality</strong> and <strong>Max cardinality </strong>boxes, specify a lower value and higher value of multiplicity.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20dfdb34-e80f-4cc9-946c-0da8b0d206fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to create the self Restriction, select the <strong>Is Self restriction</strong> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select <strong>OK</strong>. The Restriction will be edited according to the edited setting.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edited Restriction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Editing Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="618d0b73-a5bf-43c4-8ab5-420fbd2711e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">664743434</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Restriction">Restriction</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Restrictions" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Removing+Restrictions">Removing Restrictions</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478260 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00105: Effective IRI meta-property

- page_id: `170478260`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478260/Effective+IRI+meta-property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:51.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics > IRI tagged value
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

335240202

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

335240204

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

335240203

The effective IRI meta-property provides a derived absolute IRI used for OWL export on an element.

All valid elements like Classes, data types, or properties show effective meta-property for an IRI in the Specification window. This meta-property is visible in the **Standard** and **Expert** properties filters and always shows absolute IRI value.

For Existential and Universal property constraints, an IRI is the value of the property constraints.

| IRI tagged value | Function of effective meta-property for an IRI value |
| --- | --- |
| Relative IRI | Generates an absolute IRI value (not applicable for Packages). |
| Absolute IRI | Uses an absolute IRI value. |
| Blank or null IRI | Generates an absolute IRI value. |

#### INFO: Information

Information

- Relative IRI is a shortened IRI name after a hash (#) or the last slash (/).
- Absolute IRI is a full IRI which is the namespace with a relative IRI separated by a hash (#) or slash (/).

335240201

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='IRI tagged value' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Synchronize UML Package URI and Resource IRI' space='MCM2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478593 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00106: Enable Auto Diagram Assignment

- page_id: `170478593`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478593/Enable+Auto+Diagram+Assignment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:55.220+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > AutoStyler Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Enable Auto Diagram Assignment** tool is very powerful and efficient. It allows you to save time by automatically setting a class and its related elements as a defining diagram. All instances of the class and its related elements outside of this new diagram will be hyperlinked to this new defining diagram. This tool is perfect for saving time whenever you're dealing with multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='CCMP2024xR1'] at once.

To use the **Enable Auto Diagram Assignment** function

1. Select your [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD200'] .
2. Click Create Diagram.
3. Select the correct diagram according to your package .
4. Make sure you name this new diagram the same as the class you're working with.
5. Right click on the empty diagram and select AutoStyler > Enable auto diagram assignment .
6. Drag and drop your [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='CCMP2024xR1'] into the empty diagram.

This will automatically set any class that you drop in this diagram to the new defining diagram for that class, meaning that every other instance of that same class in any other diagram will become hyperlinked to this new diagram that you made. It essentially works the same as **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Set Defining Diagram' space='MCM2024xR1']** except the part where you have to left-click on the class and manually set it as the defining diagram, with **Enable Auto Diagram Assignment** it does this automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Showing how to select the Enable Auto Diagram Assignment option.

Note: Any related class with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enable Auto Diagram Assignment' space='MCM2024xR1'] function applied will immediately become the defining diagram for all instances of that class in every other diagram. Thus every instance of that class will have a hyperlink to the diagram with the enable auto diagram assignment function applied to it. Think of it as an automatic [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Set Defining Diagram' space='MCM2024xR1']function, any related class will immediately become the defining diagram for that class.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Enable Auto Diagram Assignment</strong> tool is very powerful and efficient. It allows you to save time by automatically setting a class and its related elements as a defining diagram. All instances of the class and its related elements outside of this new diagram will be hyperlinked to this new defining diagram. This tool is perfect for saving time whenever you're dealing with multiple <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CCMP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> at once.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use the <strong>Enable Auto Diagram Assignment</strong> function</p><hr /><ol><li>Select your <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD200" ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Create Diagram.</strong></li><li>Select the correct diagram according to your <strong>package</strong>.</li><li>Make sure you name this new diagram the same as the class you're working with.</li><li>Right click on the empty diagram and select <strong>AutoStyler</strong> &gt; <strong>Enable auto diagram assignment</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop your <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CCMP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> into the empty diagram.</li></ol><p>This will automatically set any class that you drop in this diagram to the new defining diagram for that class, meaning that every other instance of that same class in any other diagram will become hyperlinked to this new diagram that you made. It essentially works the same as <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Set Defining Diagram" /></ac:link></strong> except the part where you have to left-click on the class and manually set it as the defining diagram, with <strong>Enable Auto Diagram Assignment</strong> it does this automatically. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250" ac:width="387"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BetterQualityAutoStylerToEnableAutoDiagramAssignment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Enable Auto Diagram Assignment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: center;">Showing how to select the Enable Auto Diagram Assignment option.</h4><p>Note: Any related class with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Enable Auto Diagram Assignment" /></ac:link> function applied will immediately become the defining diagram for all instances of that class in every other diagram. Thus every instance of that class will have a hyperlink to the diagram with the enable auto diagram assignment function applied to it. Think of it as an automatic <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Set Defining Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Set Defining Diagram ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>function, any related class will immediately become the defining diagram for that class.</p><p><br /></p>
````

```text
Declaration(Class(:PrincipleResidentOfWhiteHouse))
      Declaration(Class(:US-President))
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :PrincipleResidentOfWhiteHouse "Principle Resident of White House"@en)
      EquivalentClasses(:PrincipleResidentOfWhiteHouse :US-President)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :US-President "US President"@en)
) ]]>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478179 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00108: Equivalent classes

- page_id: `170478179`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478179/Equivalent+classes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:48.842+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1285252119

1285252121

1285252120

The Concept Modeler can specify equivalence between two classes, import equivalent classes from OWL, and export equivalent classes to OWL. Class equivalence expresses a generalization relationship stereotyped as **«Equivalent Class»**. The Concept Modeler will draw this with a double-headed arrow.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two equivalent classes in Concept Modeler.

From the figure above, the equivalence class relationship, identified by the **«Equivalent Class»** stereotype, defines that the two classes are semantically equivalent to each other.

For more information on how to create equivalent classes, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating equivalent classes' space='MCM2024xR1'].

1285252118

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating equivalent classes' space='MCM2024xR1']

Class equivalence expresses a generalization relationship stereotyped as <strong>«Equivalent Class»</strong>. The Concept Modeler will draw this with a double-headed arrow.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Two Equivalent Classes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Equivalent classes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Two equivalent classes in Concept Modeler.</h6><p>From the figure above, the equivalence class relationship, identified by the <strong>«Equivalent Class»</strong> stereotype, defines that the two classes are semantically equivalent to each other.</p><p>For more information on how to create equivalent classes, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent classes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create Equivalent Classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d30de2b7-e905-4577-8dd1-0714966d236c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1285252118</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Concept Modeling Semantics" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating equivalent classes" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478277 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00109: Equivalent classes in NLG

- page_id: `170478277`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478277/Equivalent+classes+in+NLG
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:52.106+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

88168761

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

88168763

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

88168762

When there are two equivalent classes:

- If classes do not share the same name, one class will show **Equivalent to** and the other will show **See**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Classes with different names in the Natural Language Glossary.

- If both classes share the same name, it will appear as one class with all the properties from both classes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Classes with the same name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two equivalent classes appear as one class with all their properties displayed.

The following information explains how to determine which Class shows **Equivalent to** or **See**.

- If only one class has documentation:
  - This class will show Equivalent to and list the rest of the classes it is equivalent to.
  - The rest of the classes will show See with the link going back to the class that shows Equivalent to .
- More than one class has documentation:
  - First class with documentation in alphabetical order will show Equivalent to and list rest of the classes it is equivalent to.
  - The rest of the classes will show See with the link going back to the class that shows Equivalent to .
- None of the classes have documentation: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - First class in alphabetical order will show Equivalent to and list rest of the classes it is equivalent to.
  - The rest of the classes will show See with the link going back to the class that shows Equivalent to .

###### The merging of definitions of the equivalent classes when more than one class has documentation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The class that shows Equivalent to lists all properties and annotations from all the classes it is equivalent to.
- The class that shows See does not show any of the properties or annotations.
- All same named classes show (*package**name*) to differentiate each same named class.

###### Each different package name assigned to each class having the same name.

88168759

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c64f5aae-3940-493c-aef3-ed3a8ee56fdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">88168761</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c58cf63-e43b-4778-85e7-a352108d9b20"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">88168763</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea2e8b92-721b-4821-9705-cdc83570b62a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">88168762</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;">When there are two equivalent classes:</p><ul style="text-align: center;"><li><p style="text-align: left;">If classes do not share the same name, one class will show <strong>Equivalent to</strong> and the other will show <strong>See</strong>.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Classes with Different Names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Equivalent classes in NLG" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Classes with different names in the Natural Language Glossary.</h6><ul style="text-align: center;"><li><p style="text-align: left;">If both classes share the same name, it will appear as one class with all the properties from both classes.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Classes with Same Name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Equivalent classes in NLG" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;">            Classes with the same name.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Classes with Same Name and All Properties.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Equivalent classes in NLG" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">           Two equivalent classes appear as one class with all their properties displayed.</h6><p>The following information explains how to determine which Class shows <strong>Equivalent to</strong> or <strong>See</strong>.</p><ul><li>If only one class has documentation:<ul><li>This class will show <strong>Equivalent to</strong> and list the rest of the classes it is equivalent to.</li><li>The rest of the classes will show <strong>See</strong> with the link going back to the class that shows <strong>Equivalent to</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>More than one class has documentation:<ul><li>First class with documentation in alphabetical order will show <strong>Equivalent to</strong> and list rest of the classes it is equivalent to.</li><li>The rest of the classes will show <strong>See</strong> with the link going back to the class that shows <strong>Equivalent to</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>None of the classes have documentation:<ul><li>First class in alphabetical order will show <strong>Equivalent to</strong> and list rest of the classes it is equivalent to.</li><li>The rest of the classes will show <strong>See</strong> with the link going back to the class that shows <strong>Equivalent to</strong>.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Merging Equivalent Classes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Equivalent classes in NLG" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;">The merging of definitions of the equivalent classes when more than one class has documentation.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6adeb45b-8e64-458b-956a-b775f19b9c5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The class that shows <strong>Equivalent to</strong> lists all properties and annotations from all the classes it is equivalent to.</li><li>The class that shows <strong>See</strong> does not show any of the properties or annotations.</li><li>All same named classes show <strong>(<em>package</em><em> name</em>)</strong> to differentiate each same named class.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Same Named Classes under Packages.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Equivalent classes in NLG" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Each different package name assigned to each class having the same name.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecdedb9d-05c9-476a-8e75-05737d3d7f1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">88168759</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Concept Modeling Semantics" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478196 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00110: Equivalent properties

- page_id: `170478196`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478196/Equivalent+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:49.237+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1285051684

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1285051687

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1285051686

You can make two or more properties equivalent to each other by applying the stereotype **«Equivalent Property»** to the target property and the tagged value “**equivalent to**” the equivalent property.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- An existential or universal quantification restriction cannot have or be an equivalent property, although the property it restricts can .
- A sub-property can have or be an equivalent property.

The following figure shows the equivalent properties in a diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Equivalent properties in Concept Modeler.

In the example, the property “has mother” is equivalent to the property “has mom”.

The Concept Modeler allows you to represent, import, and export equivalent properties in a model.

For more information on how to create equivalent properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating equivalent properties' space='MCM2024xR1'].

1285051683

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating equivalent properties' space='MCM2024xR1']

## PAGE 00111: Equivalent property

- page_id: `170478402`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478402/Equivalent+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:58.800+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > UML to Equivalent OWL in OWL Functional Syntax
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

5654974

5654976

5654975

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Equivalent properties.

```text
Declaration(Class(:FemalePerson))
      Declaration(Class(:MalePerson))
      Declaration(Class(:Person))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:hasMom))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:hasMother))
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :FemalePerson "Female Person"@en)
      SubClassOf(:FemalePerson :Person)
      DisjointClasses(:FemalePerson :MalePerson)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :MalePerson "Male Person"@en)
      SubClassOf(:MalePerson :Person)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :Person "Person"@en)
      SubClassOf(:Person ObjectIntersectionOf(ObjectMaxCardinality(1 :hasMom :FemalePerson)
      ObjectMinCardinality(1 :hasMom :FemalePerson)))
      SubClassOf(:Person ObjectIntersectionOf(ObjectMaxCardinality(1 :hasMother :FemalePerson) ObjectMinCardinality(1 :hasMother :FemalePerson)))
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :hasMom "has mom"@en)
      EquivalentObjectProperties(:hasMom :hasMother)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:hasMom :Person)
      ObjectPropertyRange(:hasMom :FemalePerson)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :hasMother "has mother"@en)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:hasMother :Person)
      ObjectPropertyRange(:hasMother :FemalePerson)
)]]>
```

5654972

````html
      Declaration(Class(:MalePerson))
      Declaration(Class(:Person))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:hasMom))
      Declaration(ObjectProperty(:hasMother))

      ObjectPropertyDomain(:hasMom :Person)
      ObjectPropertyRange(:hasMom :FemalePerson)
      AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:label :hasMother "has mother"@en)
      ObjectPropertyDomain(:hasMother :Person)
      ObjectPropertyRange(:hasMother :FemalePerson)
)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc2d5288-8195-4315-b008-d877f1542f3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">5654972</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML to Equivalent OWL in OWL Functional Syntax" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478204 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00112: Existential quantification constraint

- page_id: `170478204`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478204/Existential+quantification+constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:49.550+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics > Property restrictions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Subsetting Property and Multiplicity

543649269

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Subsetting Property and Multiplicity

543649271

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Subsetting Property and Multiplicity

543649270

A property is not limited to a minimum and a maximum cardinality (known as multiplicity) for just one type. A property can have a multiplicity for a superclass, while at the same time having a more specific multiplicity for one or more subclasses of that superclass. This constraint is known as an *existential quantification*(∃) or qualified constraint. This type of constraint is an assertion that, among other possible values, the number of values of one of these subclasses is between some minimum and maximum cardinality. Adding an existential quantification constraint does not define a new property, rather it constrains an existing property. Note that an existential quantification constraint must have a minimum cardinality of at least one in order to meet the definition of “existential” for the constraint.

In the concept modeling interpretation of UML, subsetting a property without giving the new property a different name (or leaving off the new property name altogether) creates an existential quantification constraint. As *{subsets}* with an omitted name is not well defined in UML, in the concept modeling profile it is used to state that a subset of values must meet the stated cardinality and type constraints of the subsetting property. It does not create a new property, although it does create a context in which this constraint holds: *the owning class* and *its subclasses*.

The next diagram shows an existential quantification constraint on the global property “*is conferred by*” (from the property holder *“Thing”).* The multiplicity is such that at least one of the instances of the property constraint must be one of the types in the union.

#### NOTE: Unnamed Property

Unnamed Property

The property adding the constraint is unnamed. An unnamed property is equivalent, in this case, to naming this property the same as the property being constrained (“is conferred by” from the property holder “Thing”).

[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']

**An existential qualification constraint on property '*is conferred by*'.**

#### NOTE: Multiplicity

Multiplicity

- In the Concept Modeler, the existential quantification constraint of a property must have a minimum multiplicity of at least one. If the minimum multiplicity of a property that restricts another property is, for example, 0..5 or 0..*, the Concept Modeler will adjust it to 1..5 or 1..*.
- Multiplicity values of *, 0..*, and Unspecified all mean the same thing.

#### INFO: Subsetting Property and Multiplicity

Subsetting Property and Multiplicity

Modeling a UML property as a subsetting property that has 0 lower multiplicity and the «Anything» type now shows the warning message. The figure below sheds some light on the subject.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After following the orange arrows in the figure above, the notification window will show you a WARN message regarding the change from 1..* to 0..*, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

543649268

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Universal quantification constraint' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cascading restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']

A property can have a multiplicity for a superclass, while at the same time having a more specific multiplicity for one or more subclasses of that superclass. This constraint is known as an <em>existential quantification </em>(∃) or qualified constraint. This type of constraint is an assertion that, among other possible values, the number of values of one of these subclasses is between some minimum and maximum cardinality. Adding an existential quantification constraint does not define a new property, rather it constrains an existing property. Note that an existential quantification constraint must have a minimum cardinality of at least one in order to meet the definition of “existential” for the constraint.</p><p>In the concept modeling interpretation of UML, subsetting a property without giving the new property a different name (or leaving off the new property name altogether) creates an existential quantification constraint. As <em>{subsets}</em> with an omitted name is not well defined in UML, in the concept modeling profile it is used to state that a subset of values must meet the stated cardinality and type constraints of the subsetting property. It does not create a new property, although it does create a context in which this constraint holds: <em>the owning class</em> and <em>its subclasses</em>.</p><p>The next diagram shows an existential quantification constraint on the global property “<em>is conferred by</em>” (from the property holder <em>“Thing”).</em> The multiplicity is such that at least one of the instances of the property constraint must be one of the types in the union.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f63dda7-8eab-4ffd-97df-ecc86ef0b32a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Unnamed Property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The property adding the constraint is unnamed. An unnamed property is equivalent, in this case, to naming this property the same as the property being constrained (“is conferred by” from the property holder “Thing”).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-23 11:42:12.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Existential quantification constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-23 11:42:21.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Existential quantification constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>An existential qualification constraint on property '<em>is conferred by</em>'.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b15abd-7329-4ce4-9308-db85ca1edd0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Multiplicity</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>In the Concept Modeler, the existential quantification constraint of a property must have a minimum multiplicity of at least one. If the minimum multiplicity of a property that restricts another property is, for example, 0..5 or 0..*, the Concept Modeler will adjust it to 1..5 or 1..*.</li><li>Multiplicity values of *, 0..*, and Unspecified all mean the same thing.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e836d664-4211-4720-b05d-69a8ffd3973a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Subsetting Property and Multiplicity</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Modeling a UML property as a subsetting property that has 0 lower multiplicity and the «Anything» type now shows the warning message. The figure below sheds some light on the subject.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="h6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Existential quantification constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After following the orange arrows in the figure above, the notification window will show you a WARN message regarding the change from 1..* to 0..*, as shown below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="h5.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Existential quantification constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23b927ae-f73e-4061-b13e-aae73d8bee06"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">543649268</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Concept Modeling Semantics" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Property restrictions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Universal quantification constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Cascading restrictions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00113: Existing Project Migration

- page_id: `170478676`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478676/Existing+Project+Migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-31T14:19:10.230+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Available From 19.0

1188210937

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Available From 19.0

1188210948

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Available From 19.0

1188210938

GreenAvailable From 19.0

As new features are added to the modeling tool, existing projects may become outdated and will need to be migrated to be compatible with new capabilities. This section contains documentation to aid in the existing project's migration.

1188210936

**Related Pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53b3601b-440f-4e5b-9647-6a56de14a7fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1188210937</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0367b73c-3a43-4994-9bca-a3f82988bb12"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1188210948</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="946c973b-d7fc-4460-bcf8-f0613fe05684"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1188210938</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="status" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59255478-9e1c-4ef9-ad0d-c38f5d101600"><ac:parameter ac:name="colour">Green</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Available From 19.0</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>A<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">s new features are added to the modeling tool, existing projects may become outdated and will need to be migrated to be compatible with new capabilities. This section contains documentation to aid in the existing project's migration.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad849c0d-bf59-4135-8a90-776c2d8b6744"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1188210936</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4930b56a-9dc0-4e9e-9e62-3804fbea3a7f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478735 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00114: Experimental Features

- page_id: `170478735`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478735/Experimental+Features
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:12.373+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

324741820

324741841

324741831

With the modeling tool's updated version, the **Experimental Features** option plays an important role in the Concept Modeling diagrams. When the option is enabled, you can import individuals, the modeling tool will apply the «Individual List» stereotype. Otherwise, the modeling tool will ignore the applied stereotype. Additionally, when the option is enabled, you can create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association class' space='MCM2024xR1'] between Class elements, create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Phase' space='MCM2024xR1'] elements, and create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Role' space='MCM2024xR1'] elements in your diagram.

**These experimental features will become permanent after usability testing. While these features are still experimental, this option needs to enabled.**

To enable the experimental features option

1. In the main-menu, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog will open.
2. Click on Concept Modeling. Set the Enable experimental features field to true. [ATTACHMENT filename='EnvironmentOptions.PNG']
3. Now you create an Association Class, a Phase element, and a Role element with no errors.

324741819

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association class' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9f3c9d5-f64c-456a-96b9-0c3e05578116"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">324741820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="572d04ad-3e56-4d5f-9b9f-85a7d39a667a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">324741841</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aab4746e-1bd9-46c0-93c8-140b81bc9875"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">324741831</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>With the modeling tool's updated version, the <strong>Experimental Features</strong> option plays an important role in the Concept Modeling diagrams. When the option is enabled, you can import individuals, the modeling tool will apply the «Individual List» stereotype. Otherwise, the modeling tool will ignore the applied stereotype. Additionally, when the option is enabled, you can create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association class" /></ac:link> between Class elements, create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Phase" /></ac:link> elements, and create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Role" /></ac:link> elements in your diagram.</p><p><strong>These experimental features will become permanent after usability testing. While these features are still experimental, this option needs to enabled.</strong></p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the experimental features option</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main-menu, select <strong>Options &gt; Environment</strong>. The Environment Options dialog will open.</li><li>Click on <strong>Concept Modeling.</strong> Set the <strong>Enable experimental features</strong> field to true.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="EnvironmentOptions.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Experimental Features" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Now you create an Association Class, a Phase element, and a Role element with no errors.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef8b0e99-c586-4b71-8340-936e764b545f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">324741819</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usage" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Association class" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00115: Exploring the Natural Language Glossary

- page_id: `170479161`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`

- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:28.341+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with the Natural Language Glossary
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alphabetical Index Locked

1216667722

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alphabetical Index Locked

1216667735

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alphabetical Index Locked

1216667724

#### INFO: Alphabetical Index Locked

Alphabetical Index Locked

The Alphabetical Index is locked, which means that you can see the list of classes and/or properties, the search bar, and the title of the Natural Language Glossary as you are scrolling through it.

##### Searching for classes or properties

To search through the Natural Language Glossary

1. Find the Search bar at the top of your NLG.
2. Start typing the class or property you want to find. E.g. Boss . As you type, you will see highlighted variations for what you are searching. [ATTACHMENT filename='bos.png']
3. After selecting the class or property you wish to find, click Enter, and you will see the result highlighted in orange .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Suppress Model-Generated Definitions option

The **Suppress Model-Generated Definitions** option suppresses the orange, highlighted box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The orange box highlighting the Model-Generated Definition.**

Selecting the option results in the following:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The Suppress Model-Generated option is selected.**

1216667712

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478848 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00116: Exporting a concept model from TWCloud to an OWL ontology

- page_id: `170478848`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478848/Exporting+a+concept+model+from+TWCloud+to+an+OWL+ontology
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:57.390+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Adding a concept model to Teamwork Cloud and export it as an OWL ontology
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1292478106

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1292478108

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1292478107

When exporting your model to an OWL ontology, you can let the Concept Modeler export it to the previous OWL export location or your selected directory. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting destination for the OWL export folder' space='MCM2024xR1'] to learn more about the OWL export location options.

If the option **Always prompt for a file destination when exporting OWL** in the **Project Options** dialog is enabled, the Concept Modeler will always prompt you to select an OWL export file location every time you export the concept model. The following figure below shows the prompt for OWL export folder option is enabled (set as **true**).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Prompt for an OWL export file destination option.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You do not have to lock the concept model to export it to an OWL ontology.

The following steps explain how to export a concept model that you have added to TWCloud.

To export a concept model in TWCloud to an OWL ontology

1. Right-click a concept model in the Containment tree.
2. Selct **Concept Modeling** > **Export Model to OWL** to export the concept model. NoteIf the prompt for export location is either enabled or disabled, and you never export the project to an OWL ontology before, a dialog will open to prompt you to select a desired location. This location will be set as default.When saving new export location and package is unlocked, Concept Modeler will try to lock it and show message in notification window. If locks cannot be obtained, due to package being locked by another user, a message will show in notification window, and export location will not be saved, but concept model will still export.If the prompt for export location is enabled, you have exported a project before, and the location is valid, a dialog will open prompting you to select either the previous location or a new location.If the prompt for export location is enabled, you have exported a project before, and the location is invalid, an error will open in the Notification Window, and a dialog will open prompting you to select a new location.If the prompt for export location is disabled, you have exported a project before, and the location is valid, the Concept Modeler will export it directly to the location.If the prompt for export location is disabled, you have exported a project before, and the export location is invalid, an error will open in the Notification Window, and the Concept Modeler will prompt you to select another location.

When you would like to select multiple packages, you must right-click on one of the packages to export. You will always receive a prompt for file destination which is applied to all of your packages and the same file location will be loaded to all the packages. You are able to select different file destinations for each package, but the software always saves the same starting location for each package.

The error that appears in the Notification Window when trying to export the concept model to an OWL ontology can be caused by entering an incorrect path name or the path name to the previous export location does not exist anymore. If you encounter this type of error, you need to open the **fileExportPath** tagged value in the model’s **Specification** dialog and correct the path name, or select another location.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The OWL export destination directory in the model’s Specification dialog.

For more information about changing the OWL export location, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1'].

1292487686

**Related pages**

- Usage
- Adding a concept model to Teamwork Cloud and export it as an OWL ontology

See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting destination for the OWL export folder" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OWL Export Folder]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more about the OWL export location options.</p><p>If the option <strong>Always prompt for a file destination when exporting OWL</strong> in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog is enabled, the Concept Modeler will always prompt you to select an OWL export file location every time you export the concept model. The following figure below shows the prompt for OWL export folder option is enabled (set as <strong>true</strong>).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 13:30:9.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting a concept model from TWCloud to an OWL ontology" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Prompt for an OWL export file destination option.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35ed08aa-7f42-4f84-82b9-deb317a8d222"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You do not have to lock the concept model to export it to an OWL ontology.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following steps explain how to export a concept model that you have added to TWCloud.</p><p>To export a concept model in TWCloud to an OWL ontology</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a concept model in the Containment tree.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Selct <strong>Concept Modeling</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Model to OWL</strong> to export the concept model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b2c86f7-c72f-414b-a0d1-e62a275d0f6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the prompt for export location is either enabled or disabled, and you never export the project to an OWL ontology before, a dialog will open to prompt you to select a desired location. This location will be set as default.</li><li>When saving new export location and package is unlocked, Concept Modeler will try to lock it and show message in notification window. If locks cannot be obtained, due to package being locked by another user, a message will show in notification window, and export location will not be saved, but concept model will still export.</li><li>If the prompt for export location is enabled, you have exported a project before, and the location is valid, a dialog will open prompting you to select either the previous location or a new location.</li><li>If the prompt for export location is enabled, you have exported a project before, and the location is invalid, an error will open in the Notification Window, and a dialog will open prompting you to select a new location.</li><li>If the prompt for export location is disabled, you have exported a project before, and the location is valid, the Concept Modeler will export it directly to the location.</li><li>If the prompt for export location is disabled, you have exported a project before, and the export location is invalid, an error will open in the Notification Window, and the Concept Modeler will prompt you to select another location.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>When you would like to select multiple packages, you must right-click on one of the packages to export. You will always receive a prompt for file destination which is applied to all of your packages and the same file location will be loaded to all the packages. You are able to select different file destinations for each package, but the software always saves the same starting location for each package.</p><p>The error that appears in the Notification Window when trying to export the concept model to an OWL ontology can be caused by entering an incorrect path name or the path name to the previous export location does not exist anymore. If you encounter this type of error, you need to open the <strong>fileExportPath</strong> tagged value in the model’s <strong>Specification</strong> dialog and correct the path name, or select another location.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 13:31:50.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting a concept model from TWCloud to an OWL ontology" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The OWL export destination directory in the model’s Specification dialog.</h6><p>For more information about changing the OWL export location, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exporting a Concept Model to an OWL ontology]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4aa297c1-b6a8-4861-ae6d-a7575a9bb562"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1292487686</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Adding+a+concept+model+to+Teamwork+Cloud+and+export+it+as+an+OWL+ontology">Adding a concept model to Teamwork Cloud and export it as an OWL ontology</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
