<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479062 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00196: Removing Restrictions

- page_id: `170479062`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479062/Removing+Restrictions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:22.843+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with restrictions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

587853548

587853560

587853550

To remove a universal quantification constraint

1. Right-click on a redefining property.
2. Select Concept Modeling > Remove specification that objects may only be from type '{}' . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Removing a universal quantification constraint from a property by using the "Remove specification that objects may only be from type 'Dog'"command.

To remove an existential quantification constraint

1. Right-click on a subsetting property.
2. Do one of the following
  - Select Concept Modeling > Remove specification that some objects must be from type '{}' for the lower multiplicity of 1.
  - Select Concept Modeling > Remove specification of multiplicity for type '{}' for the lower multiplicity of 2 or more.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Removing an existential quantification constraint from a property by using the "Remove specification that some objects must be from type 'Dog'"command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Removing an existential quantification constraint from a property by using the "Remove specification of multiplicity for type 'Dog'" command.

587853547

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing Restrictions' space='MCM2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478589 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00197: Repair Styles

- page_id: `170478589`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478589/Repair+Styles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:55.149+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > AutoStyler Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Repair Styles** function is used to highlight the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MCM2024xR1'] you have applied the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Set Defining Diagram' space='MCM2024xR1']** function to. The **Repair Styles** function will allow you to quickly see which classes have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='SYSMLP2024xR1'] attached. The **Repair Styles** function is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing AutoStyler' space='MCM2024xR1']and allows you to change the color and any other default feature of **Repair Styles** there is to suit your purposes. Another aspect of the **Repair Styles** function is it displays the package names of the classes the function has been applied to.

To use the **Repair Styles** function

1. Right click on the class.
2. Click AutoStyler > Repair Styles.

This will allow linked classes to be visibly identifiable by becoming grey and faded-out, although these colors can be customized.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Showing how to select the Repair Styles option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Repair Styles</strong> function is used to highlight the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you have applied the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Set Defining Diagram" /></ac:link></strong> function to. The <strong>Repair Styles</strong> function will allow you to quickly see which classes have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[hyperlink]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> attached. The <strong>Repair Styles</strong> function is also <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing AutoStyler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customizable ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and allows you to change the color and any other default feature of <strong>Repair Styles</strong> there is to suit your purposes. Another aspect of the <strong>Repair Styles</strong> function is it displays the package names of the classes the function has been applied to.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use the <strong>Repair Styles</strong> function</p><hr /><ol><li>Right click on the class.</li><li>Click <strong>AutoStyler</strong> &gt; <strong>Repair Styles.</strong></li></ol><p>This will allow linked classes to be visibly identifiable by becoming grey and faded-out, although these colors can be customized.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250" ac:width="390"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BetterQualityAutoStylerToRepairStyles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Repair Styles" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: center;">Showing how to select the Repair Styles option.</h4><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00214: Restrictions

- page_id: `170478697`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Available From 19.0 SP3

1283606997

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Available From 19.0 SP3

1283607008

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Available From 19.0 SP3

1283607007

GreenAvailable From 19.0 SP3

When migrating existing projects into a newer version of the modeling tool, an informative message dialog asking about your existing model's restrictions will appear.

This message dialog appears when opening your existing project in the newer version.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Informative message dialog about your existing model's restrictions.

If you click **Yes,** the Notification Window will show you which elements have the Restriction stereotype applied to them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Notification Window showing which elements have the Restriction stereotype.

If you click **No,** the Notification Window will show you the following warning message:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Notification Window stating that the Restriction stereotype is not applied and warning that it may cause future problems.

1283606996

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']

modeling tool, an informative message dialog asking about your existing model's restrictions will appear.</p><p><span>This message dialog appears when opening your existing project in the newer version.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="restricitons.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Informative message dialog about your existing model's restrictions. </h6><p>If you click <strong>Yes,</strong> the Notification Window will show you which elements have the Restriction stereotype applied to them.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NW.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Notification Window showing which elements have the Restriction stereotype.</h6><p>If you click <strong>No,</strong> the Notification Window will show you the following warning message: </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NWN.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Restrictions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Notification Window stating that the Restriction stereotype is not applied and warning that it may cause future problems. </h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="978990bc-d4c9-49b7-8252-2baf09e96476"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1283606996</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usage" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478175 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00215: Role

- page_id: `170478175`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478175/Role
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:48.688+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics > Advanced Modeling Patterns > Facets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1611131516

1611131527

1611131526

A «Role» is a kind of «Facet» that is played by exactly one identifiable thing. It is the way some identifiable thing participates in relationships with other concepts, and may specify the necessary conditions for playing the «Role» concept.

When the necessary conditions for playing a «Role» concept are met, the identifiable thing will be a member of that concept. When the necessary conditions for playing a «Role» concept are not met, the identifiable thing will not be a member of that concept.

When it is necessary for an individual to have properties that are existentially dependent on a relationship, a role being played can be treated as a separate qua entity (i.e., a thing in a role). When it is not necessary to have properties that are existentially dependent on a relationship, the identifiable thing playing the role can be a member of the role concept.

1611131515

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It is the way some identifiable thing participates in relationships with other concepts, and may specify the necessary conditions for playing the «Role» concept.</p><p>When the necessary conditions for playing a «Role» concept are met, the identifiable thing will be a member of that concept. When the necessary conditions for playing a «Role» concept are not met, the identifiable thing will not be a member of that concept.</p><p>When it is necessary for an individual to have properties that are existentially dependent on a relationship, a role being played can be treated as a separate qua entity (i.e., a thing in a role). When it is not necessary to have properties that are existentially dependent on a relationship, the identifiable thing playing the role can be a member of the role concept.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74cdc115-fc20-4932-94c3-114799cc0f2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1611131515</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Concept Modeling Semantics" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479137 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00216: Selecting a Preferred Annotation Property for a UML Comment or Annotation

- page_id: `170479137`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479137/Selecting+a+Preferred+Annotation+Property+for+a+UML+Comment+or+Annotation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:59.835+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with annotations > Working with annotation properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Locked Elements

1048929335

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Locked Elements

1048929337

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Locked Elements

1048929336

To select a preferred annotation property tagged value for an existing «Annotation» of an element

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the left pane of the dialog, select General and Concept Modeling . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 10:2:29.png']
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 10:3:42.png'] . The Select Property «Annotation Property» dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 10:4:0.png']
4. Click the List tab and select an annotation property for the comments, for example, definition .
5. Click OK . The selected annotation property tagged value definition will be created as the current preferred annotation property for all comments/annotations in your model. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 10:5:17.png'] After clicking OK, a progress bar will appear.

#### INFO: Locked Elements

Locked Elements

If your project is a Teamwork Cloud (TWC) project, Concept Modeler will attempt to lock the project’s elements.

- If any of the elements cannot be locked, whether it is locked by another user, then the dialog box with the OK button will say “ Cannot lock all elements for edit to allow preferred annotations to be used as element documentation. You may refer to the Lock View tab to see what still needs to be locked. ”
- Furthermore, an additional message will appear in the notification window saying “ The preferred annotation property change has been reverted. ”

After these two messages will appear, the preferred annotation property will revert back to its previous value.

The following example shows you how to change the tagged value **definition** to an unspecified preferred annotation property.

To change a current preferred annotation property tagged value to an unspecified preferred annotation property tagged value

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the left pane of the dialog, select General and Concept Modeling .
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 10:10:59.png'] next to Preferred annotation property . The Select Property «Annotation Property» dialog opens.
4. Select <UNSPECIFIED> and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 10:11:22.png']
5. Click OK . The definition tagged value will be removed from the Preferred annotation property box, and the annotation will be moved back under the owning folder, e.g., the Agents Package.

1277786525

**Related pages**

- Usage

## PAGE 00217: Selecting an ordered list of annotation properties

- page_id: `170479173`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479173/Selecting+an+ordered+list+of+annotation+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:29.242+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with the Natural Language Glossary > Customizing your Natural Language Glossary
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1277640366

1277640369

1277640368

Concept Modeler has another feature allowing you to select an ordered list of annotation properties to be displayed in the Natural Language Glossary.

To select an ordered list of annotation properties in the Natural Language Glossary

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the tree structure on the left, expand General and select Concept Modeling .
3. Click at the right side of the Natural Language Glossary annotation property list option and select [ATTACHMENT filename='Browse for.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='Annotation Properties in Project Options Dialog.png'] The Select Property «Annotation Property» dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Select Property Dialog.png']
4. Select one or more annotation properties (hold CTRL to multi-select properties) and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Plus Sign.png'] to add the selected properties.
5. Click OK .

After you generate a report from the project that you have selected a number of annotation properties from the **Natural Language Glossary annotation property list** option, the selected annotation properties are listed in the generated Natural Language Glossary report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A section of the report showing the ordered list of annotation properties according to the selected annotation property list in the project.

1277750110

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usage' space='MCM2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478585 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00218: Set Defining Diagram

- page_id: `170478585`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478585/Set+Defining+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:55.085+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > AutoStyler Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Any [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MCM2024xR1'] that has had the **Set Defining Diagram** function applied to it will automatically become [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='SYSMLP2024xR1'] with all the instances of that class in every other diagram in the project. Double right-clicking on any one of these classes will automatically reroute you to their defining diagram.

This is really helpful for quickly navigating to the diagram you determined to be the defining diagram of that class. Furthermore, the **Set Defining Diagram** function serves to connect several instances of the same class together to make multiple diagrams more linked.

To use the **Set Defining Diagram** function

1. Right-click on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MCM2024xR1'] .
2. Click on AutoStyler > Set Defining Diagram.

This will create a hyperlink between the class you applied the function to and all the instances of that class in all the other diagrams in that singular project.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Showing how to select the Set Defining Diagram option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Any <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that has had the <strong>Set Defining Diagram</strong> function applied to it will automatically become <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[hyperlinked]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with all the instances of that class in every other diagram in the project. Double right-clicking on any one of these classes will automatically reroute you to their defining diagram.</p><p>This is really helpful for quickly navigating to the diagram you determined to be the defining diagram of that class. Furthermore, the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Set Defining Diagram</strong> function serves to connect several instances of the same class together to make multiple diagrams more linked.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use the <strong>Set Defining Diagram</strong> function</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click on <strong>AutoStyler</strong> &gt; <strong>Set Defining Diagram. </strong></li></ol><p>This will create a hyperlink between the class you applied the function to and all the instances of that class in all the other diagrams in that singular project.</p><h4 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="BetterQualityAutoStylerToSetDefiningDiagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Set Defining Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Showing how to select the Set Defining Diagram option.</h4><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00219: Setting destination for the OWL export folder

- page_id: `170478794`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478794/Setting+destination+for+the+OWL+export+folder
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:56.290+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

241442528

241442530

241442529

If you would like to learn more about OWL export folder, please read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OWL export folder' space='MCM2024xR1'].

To select a destination folder for export

1. From the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the left pane of the dialog, select General and Concept Modeling .
3. Select the Always prompt for a file destination when exporting OWL option. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 13:2:49.png']
4. Click OK .

241442527

**Related pages**

- Usage
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b641df81-4b54-4ed4-a732-01c946695497"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241442528</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="008bdc7f-7c7f-41a2-8dde-7a2f75100b4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241442530</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b138f6da-b003-4951-9b8a-2b8ae8f78f11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241442529</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you would like to learn more about OWL export folder, please read <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="OWL export folder" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To select a destination folder for export</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the left pane of the dialog, select <strong>General</strong> and <strong>Concept Modeling</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Always prompt for a file destination when exporting OWL</strong> option.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 13:2:49.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting destination for the OWL export folder" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54304cbb-97ca-4e9f-b94c-1317011959a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241442527</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478796 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00220: Setting the concept model export syntax

- page_id: `170478796`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478796/Setting+the+concept+model+export+syntax
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:56.371+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology
- labels: ['concept-model-export']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: User Manual Info

241387394

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: User Manual Info

241387396

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: User Manual Info

241387395

The Concept Modeler provides many syntaxes (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML to Equivalent OWL in OWL Functional Syntax' space='MCM2024xR1'] in OWL Functional Syntax) from which you can select to export your concept model project to an OWL ontology. If you export your model without selecting a syntax, the Concept Modeler will export it using RDF/XML, which is the default syntax.

#### WARNING: User Manual Info

User Manual Info

Please note that the documentation uses OWL Functional as its OWL export syntax. Of course, you are welcome to use a different syntax.

To set the syntax to export a concept model

1. In the main menu, click **Options** > **Project**. The **Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the left column, select **General** > **Concept Modeling**.
3. Toggle the drop down menu in OWL Export Syntax and select a syntax. [ATTACHMENT filename='syntax.png']

241387393

**Related pages**

- Usage
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

Of course, you are welcome to use a different syntax.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To set the syntax to export a concept model</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</p></li><li><p>In the left column, select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">General</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Concept Modeling</strong>.</p></li><li>Toggle the drop down menu in <strong>OWL Export Syntax</strong> and <strong>select</strong> a syntax.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="syntax.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting the concept model export syntax" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56ba578a-8373-47d2-986a-2f0479f3f4a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241387393</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478804 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00221: Setting the concept model export URI style

- page_id: `170478804`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478804/Setting+the+concept+model+export+URI+style
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:56.455+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

241416844

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

241416847

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

241416846

Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) is necessary for your concept model before the model can be exported to an OWL ontology. If you would like to learn more about URI, please read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept model export URI style' space='MCM2024xR1'].

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the last character of the «Model» URI is a hash or slash and different from the selected **URI Construction Strategy** in **Concept Modeling** options, the delimiter will be removed.

To select a concept model export URI style for a MagicDraw project

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the left pane of the dialog, select General and Concept Modeling .
3. Click in the field next to URI Construction Strategy .
4. Select either**Hash URI**or**303 URI**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

241416843

**Related pages**

- Usage
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the left pane of the dialog, select <strong>General</strong> and <strong>Concept Modeling</strong>.</li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Click in the field next to </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">URI Construction Strategy</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">.</span></li><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Select either </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Hash URI</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"> or </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">303 URI</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">.</span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ProjectOptions_URIconstruction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting the concept model export URI style" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a48322f5-6569-4d98-847f-3007fb579433"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">241416843</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478807 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00222: Setting the concept model URI

- page_id: `170478807`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478807/Setting+the+concept+model+URI
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:56.545+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Unsure of URI's format?

236197399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Unsure of URI's format?

236197402

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Unsure of URI's format?

236197401

URI stands for Uniform Resource Identifier. A URI can provide identification about a location to a resource (e.g., a document, a person, an abstract thing) and a name, or both depending on the context. The URI is used as a single global identification system in the Web. On the semantic Web, not only can you use URIs to link to Web documents and access them in a Web browser, but also for real-world objects (e.g., people, cars, and even abstract ideas).

A concept model must have a valid URI before it can be exported to an OWL ontology. **If you forget to change the default URI, the notification window will open to remind you to change it when you export the concept model to an OWL ontology. If you are unsure of the URI's format, please refer to the following link that describes the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396: [http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt](http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt).**

The last part of the URI is used as the filename, and the extension for this file will be derived from the export format specified in the project options. (See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting the concept model export URI style' space='MCM2024xR1'] for export format options.)

To set the concept model URI

1. Right-click an ontology package in the Containment tree. Select Specification . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 10:41:14.png']
2. Scroll down to **URI,** or type “**URI”** in the search field at the bottom of page. NoteA default URI will be set for new concept modeling projects and newly created concept models. Updating the URI to match your ontology is recommended. For situations in which a URI has not been set, or the default has not been changed, a warning message will appear in the notification window on OWL export.
3. Click the field next to **URI**. Unsure of URI's format?A URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 [http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt](http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt) and it must comply with those syntax rules.
4. Update the URI and click OK .

236197398

**Related pages**

- Usage
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A URI can provide identification about a location to a resource (e.g., a document, a person, an abstract thing) and a name, or both depending on the context. The URI is used as a single global identification system in the Web. On the semantic Web, not only can you use URIs to link to Web documents and access them in a Web browser, but also for real-world objects (e.g., people, cars, and even abstract ideas).</p><p>A concept model must have a valid URI before it can be exported to an OWL ontology. <strong>If you forget to change the default URI, the notification window will open to remind you to change it when you export the concept model to an OWL ontology. If you are unsure of the URI's format, please refer to the following link that describes the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396: <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt" class="external-link" rel="nofollow">http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt</a>.</strong></p><p>The last part of the URI is used as the filename, and the extension for this file will be derived from the export format specified in the project options. (See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting the concept model export URI style" /></ac:link> for export format options.)</p><p><br /></p><p>To set the concept model URI</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click an ontology package in the Containment tree. Select <strong>Specification</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 10:41:14.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting the concept model URI" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p>Scroll down to <strong>URI,</strong> or type “<strong>URI”</strong> in the search field at the bottom of page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84adb5e8-58f7-425a-bb00-d5f4f4b64911"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A default URI will be set for new concept modeling projects and newly created concept models. Updating the URI to match your ontology is recommended. For situations in which a URI has not been set, or the default has not been changed, a warning message will appear in the notification window on OWL export.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the field next to <strong>URI</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="480b1a6a-83fa-4902-a5eb-53afa78ac0da"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Unsure of URI's format?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 <a href="http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt">http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt</a> and it must comply with those syntax rules.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Update the URI and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3cc00538-2d68-49d3-9d0c-9fb358c509a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">236197398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478765 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00223: Setting the OWL import catalog in MagicDraw

- page_id: `170478765`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478765/Setting+the+OWL+import+catalog+in+MagicDraw
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:55.917+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Importing an OWL ontology to a concept model
- labels: ['owl-import-catalog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Path Variables

239695659

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Path Variables

239695661

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Path Variables

239695660

The OWL import catalog must be set for a MagicDraw project if an XML catalog is used to import an ontology model.

To set the OWL import catalog to the XML catalog file for the desired external ontologies

1. Click **Options** > **Project**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **The Concept Modeler's Options menu.**
2. Select General > Concept Modeling .
3. Click in the field next to OWL Import Catalog .
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Select the XML catalog file.
6. Click Open .
7. Path VariablesThe Path variables dialog window may appear. If it does, click **Use Selected.**
8. Click OK .

239704065

**Related pages**

- Usage

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479109 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00224: Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane

- page_id: `170479109`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479109/Showing+an+annotation+in+the+Documentation+pane
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:59.573+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with annotations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1048860656

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1048860659

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1048860657

There are several ways to make an annotation for a class or a property appears in the Documentation pane any time you click the annotation in the Containment tree or in the diagram pane.

To show an annotation for a class in the Documentation pane when you click the class

1. Drag the annotation (either on the diagram or in the Containment tree) to the class. (This makes it owned by the class.) [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 9:51:28.png']
2. Click the class. The annotation will show up in the **Documentation** pane. [IMAGE alt='' src='']**The annotation owned by the class shows in the Documentation pane.**

To make an annotation for a property appear in the Documentation pane when you click the property

1. Double-click an annotation in the Containment tree to open its **Specification** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the Specification dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 9:5:51.png'] next to Annotated Element . The Select Elements dialog will open. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 9:54:56.png']
3. To remove a Selected Element, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. To add a Selected Element, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click OK . The Annotated Element of the annotation has been changed, in this example, from Item to loans . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-31 9:56:35.png']
5. Click Close to close the Specification dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If you drag more than one annotation to a class or a property, only the first created annotation will appear in the Documentation pane and in the class’ Specification window (under the Documentation/Hyperlink property).
- An annotation for a property will appear in the Documentation pane only if its annotated element is set to the property and its preferred annotation property tagged value is specified or updated.
- In this current release, only annotations that have been adjusted to show in the Documentation pane will appear in the Natural Language Glossary.

1277804102

**Related pages**

- Usage

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="282a74d9-3ad9-4a53-91a8-14e6f2b67912"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048860656</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8294dd69-1754-4231-a928-72b07180e98d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048860659</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="322e7deb-271b-49ed-b1a5-0946bc4ad54b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048860657</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are several ways to make an annotation for a class or a property appears in the Documentation pane any time you click the annotation in the Containment tree or in the diagram pane.</p><p>To show an annotation for a class in the Documentation pane when you click the class</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the annotation (either on the diagram or in the Containment tree) to the class. (This makes it owned by the class.)<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:51:28.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Click the class. The annotation will show up in the <strong>Documentation</strong> pane.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:53:5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>The annotation owned by the class shows in the Documentation pane.</strong></p></li></ol><p>To make an annotation for a property appear in the Documentation pane when you click the property</p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;"><p>Double-click an annotation in the Containment tree to open its <strong>Specification</strong> dialog.</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:13:52.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li style="text-align: left;">In the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:5:51.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to <strong>Annotated Element</strong>. The <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog will open.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:54:56.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>To remove a Selected Element, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:6:22.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. To add a Selected Element, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:6:34.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:55:43.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The Annotated Element of the annotation has been changed, in this example, from <strong>Item</strong> to <strong>loans</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-31 9:56:35.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing an annotation in the Documentation pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> to close the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d96d4132-bfca-4de1-b3bb-c04efd06993f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you drag more than one annotation to a class or a property, only the first created annotation will appear in the Documentation pane and in the class’ <strong>Specification</strong> window (under the <strong>Documentation/Hyperlink</strong> property).</li><li>An annotation for a property will appear in the <strong>Documentation</strong> pane only if its annotated element is set to the property and its preferred annotation property tagged value is specified or updated.</li><li>In this current release, only annotations that have been adjusted to show in the <strong>Documentation</strong> pane will appear in the Natural Language Glossary.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c40700b-aaa6-4c2e-8b85-17dd05b1e9a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277804102</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li></ul><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170479102 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00225: Showing annotations on the diagram

- page_id: `170479102`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170479102/Showing+annotations+on+the+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:59.471+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with annotations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1048837640

1048837643

1048837642

A model, imported or not, may contain annotations for a *class* or a *property.* However, they may not appear on the diagram pane, but you can see them in the Containment tree. The following steps will show you how to make those annotations appear on the diagram.

To show an annotation(s) for a **class** on a diagram

1. Right-click a class and select Display > Display Related Elements .
2. In the Display Related Elements dialog, select Comment > OK (see the following figure). The annotation(s) for the class will appear on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The Display Related Elements dialog.**

To show an annotation(s) for a **property** on a diagram

1. Drag an annotation(s) from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 15:20:33.png'] Dragging annotations from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.
2. Right-click the annotation(s) and select **Display** > **Display Related Elements**(in the following figure). A question dialog will open.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] **The Display Related Elements menu of a property.**
3. Click No .

The annotation(s) for the property(ies) will appear on the diagram (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The annotations for the properties showing on the diagram.**

1277817728

**Related pages**

- Usage

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76ea19a4-bdc4-4fb8-af68-48c13e16cda5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048837640</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d69e61fc-7552-4ec2-816f-dedca55191ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048837643</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6f68ab2-29c3-41da-a83c-6dda36112c97"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1048837642</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A model, imported or not, may contain annotations for a <em>class</em> or a <em>property.</em> However, they may not appear on the diagram pane, but you can see them in the Containment tree. The following steps will show you how to make those annotations appear on the diagram.</p><p>To show an annotation(s) for a <strong>class</strong> on a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a class and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog, select <strong>Comment</strong> &gt; <strong>OK</strong> (see the following figure). The annotation(s) for the class will appear on the diagram.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 15:19:12.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing annotations on the diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><strong>The Display Related Elements dialog.</strong></p><p>To show an annotation(s) for a <strong>property</strong> on a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag an annotation(s) from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 15:20:33.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing annotations on the diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dragging annotations from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.</h6></li><li><p>Right-click the annotation(s) and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements </strong>(in the following figure). A question dialog will open.<ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 15:23:27.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing annotations on the diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>The Display Related Elements menu of a property.</strong></span></p></li><li>Click <strong>No</strong>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The annotation(s) for the property(ies) will appear on the diagram (see the following figure).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-30 15:24:54.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing annotations on the diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><strong>The annotations for the properties showing on the diagram.</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93f862c2-cbd4-48f2-ac75-a8a4824674f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277817728</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478809 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00226: Specifying file export paths

- page_id: `170478809`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478809/Specifying+file+export+paths
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:56.634+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

625496013

625496016

625496015

Before exporting a model to an OWL ontology, you need to specify the file export path. The **fileExportPath** property allows you to store the file export path as a tagged value in your Concept Modeling package.

To specify a file export path to export a model to an OWL ontology

1. Right-click on a concept model that you want to export, and select Specification . The Specification window opens.
2. On the left column, select **Tags**.
3. In the tree structure, do one of the following:****
  - Double-click fileExportPath .
  - Select fileExportPath and click Create Value . [ATTACHMENT filename='File Export Path.png']
4. The Value box appears on the right side of the dialog.
5. In the Value box, specify the export path.
6. Click **Close**.

625496012

**Related pages**

- Usage
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

The <strong>Specification</strong> window opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the left column, select <strong>Tags</strong>.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the tree structure, do one of the following:<strong><br /></strong></p><ul><li>Double-click <strong>fileExportPath</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>fileExportPath</strong> and click <strong>Create Value</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="File Export Path.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying file export paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>The<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Value</strong> box appears on the right side of the dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> box, specify the export path.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="691381e7-8c13-4f81-8928-3c61b4a21309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">625496012</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478813 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00227: Specifying IRI ontology versions

- page_id: `170478813`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478813/Specifying+IRI+ontology+versions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:56.716+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

628928268

628928270

628928269

An ontology may have an ontology IRI, the ontology's identifier. The ontology may or may not also a**versionIRI**, the ontology's version identifier.

To specify an IRI ontology version to an exported concept model

1. Right-click a concept model you want to export and select Specification . The Specification window opens.
2. On the left pane, select **Tags**.
3. In the tree structure, do one of the following****
  - Double-click versionIRI .
  - Select versionIRI and click Create Value . [ATTACHMENT filename='Specifying IRI - Tags.png']
4. The Value box appears on the right side of the dialog.
5. In the Value box, specify the version for the exported concept model.
6. Click Close .

After you export the concept model, the **versionIRI** tag is displayed in the exported ontology file to show the version number.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**The versionIRI tag is displayed in the RDF file.**

628928266

**Related pages**

- Usage
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology' space='MCM2024xR1']

The Specification window opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the left pane, select <strong>Tags</strong>.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the tree structure, do one of the following<strong><br /></strong></p><ul><li>Double-click <strong>versionIRI</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>versionIRI</strong> and click <strong>Create Value</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specifying IRI - Tags.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying IRI ontology versions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>The<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Value</strong> box appears on the right side of the dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> box, specify the version for the exported concept model.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p>After you export the concept model, the <strong>versionIRI</strong> tag is displayed in the exported ontology file to show the version number.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ontology version.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying IRI ontology versions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong>The versionIRI tag is displayed in the RDF file.</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c969108e-cf97-4e15-be8d-b87c471922c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628928266</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCM2024xR1/Usage">Usage</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Exporting your concept model to an OWL ontology" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478198 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00228: Subproperty

- page_id: `170478198`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478198/Subproperty
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:39:49.318+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

73831835

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

73831838

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

73831837

A subproperty is a more specific kind of property than some other property, and a super property is a more general kind of property than some other property. For example, “has father” is a more specific property than “has parent”, and “has parent” is a more general property than either “has mother” or “has father”. In the concept modeling interpretation of UML, subsetting a property creates a subproperty when the subsetting property has a different name than the subsetted property. (See section 3.5 Existential Quantification Constraint, for when the name is the same or is omitted.) UML provides a {subsets} constraint that asserts that the values within a subsetting property are also in the set of values within a subsetted property. To stay as close to standard UML as possible, the concept modeling profile interprets a subsetting property having a different name as a subproperty.

The diagram below shows that the property “is capacity of” (owned by the class “Legal Capacity”) is a subset of the global property “is conferred on” (from the property holder “Thing”).

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In order to create a subproperty, the subsetting property must have a different name than the property it subsets.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property 'is conferred on' and subproperty 'is capacity of' having different names.

73831834

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b95bf3a-158c-4b2c-a3cc-15fca7ab90eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73831835</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d636bcb2-52cd-44ee-b148-7f6d79d2fad5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73831838</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4afbd21-ea7c-44c8-a08e-85bbf5442ab6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73831837</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A subproperty is a more specific kind of property than some other property, and a super property is a more general kind of property than some other property. For example, “has father” is a more specific property than “has parent”, and “has parent” is a more general property than either “has mother” or “has father”. In the concept modeling interpretation of UML, subsetting a property creates a subproperty when the subsetting property has a different name than the subsetted property. (See section 3.5 Existential Quantification Constraint, for when the name is the same or is omitted.) UML provides a {subsets} constraint that asserts that the values within a subsetting property are also in the set of values within a subsetted property. To stay as close to standard UML as possible, the concept modeling profile interprets a subsetting property having a different name as a subproperty.</p><p>The diagram below shows that the property “is capacity of” (owned by the class “Legal Capacity”) is a subset of the global property “is conferred on” (from the property holder “Thing”).  </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14a5ec9d-777f-4cad-b22e-a4cc37e9b373"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to create a subproperty, the subsetting property must have a different name than the property it subsets.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-23 11:37:39.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Subproperty" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="350"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-8-23 11:37:48.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Subproperty" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Property 'is conferred on' and subproperty 'is capacity of' having different names.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="986a920e-74d6-42ff-8dc8-d7549a5653ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">73831834</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Concept Modeling Semantics" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478981 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00229: Subsetted property

- page_id: `170478981`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478981/Subsetted+property
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:58.580+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Working with properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

427532232

427532234

427532233

It is often useful to go to a subsetted property to see its original definition. There are two ways in which this can be done. The first is to go to the subsetted property in the Containment tree. The second is to go to the subsetted property on a diagram.

To focus on a subsetted property in the Containment tree

1. Right-click on the subsetting property, its multiplicity, or its {subsets}. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 14:40:30.png'] A subsetting property in the Concept Modeler.
2. Select**Go To**>**Subsetted property in containment tree.** **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** The Subsetted property in the Containment tree.

The focus will jump to the subsetted property in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Concept Modeler highlights the subsetted property in the Containment tree.

To focus on a subsetted property on a diagram

1. Right-click on the subsetting property, its multiplicity, or its {subsets}. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 14:43:41.png'] A subsetting property in the Concept Modeler.
2. Select Go To > Subsetted property on diagram and select a diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-8-30 14:44:29.png'] The Subsetted property on diagram shortcut menu.

The focus will jump to the subsetted property on the selected diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Concept Modeler highlights the subsetted property on the selected diagram.

427532231

**Related page**

- Usage

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478714 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00230: Sufficient constraint

- page_id: `170478714`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478714/Sufficient+constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-24T11:40:11.205+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Usage > Existing Project Migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

GreenAvailable From 19.0 SP1

When migrating existing projects into a newer version of the modeling tool, you will see an informative message dialog asking you about your existing model's {sufficient} constraints.

This message dialog appears when opening your existing project in the newer version.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Informative message dialog about your existing model's sufficient constraints.**

If you click **Yes,** then the Notification Window will show you which elements have the **Necessary and Sufficient** stereotype applied to them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Notification Window showing which elements have the Necessary and Sufficient stereotype.**

If you click **No,** then the Notification Window will show you the following warning message shown in the next figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Notification Window saying that the Necessary and Sufficient stereotype is not applied and warning that it may cause problems.**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478261 space=MCM2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00231: Synchronize UML Package URI and Resource IRI

- page_id: `170478261`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478261/Synchronize+UML+Package+URI+and+Resource+IRI
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-31T14:18:45.167+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Concept Modeling Semantics > IRI tagged value
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Available From 19.0 SP2

1236311767

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Available From 19.0 SP2

1236311779

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Available From 19.0 SP2

1236311769

A UML Package URI is a **Package** property value which is visible in the MagicDraw specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The «Resource» IRI is a **Package Tags** value which is visible in the MagicDraw specification window's **Tags** section.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Both values have the same meaning, but their values could be different. To avoid confusion, the modeling tool synchronizes both values.

There are several cases to consider:

- When creating or updating a UML Package URI value, the «Resource» IRI tagged value will be updated to be the same as the URI value.
- When removing a UML Package URI value, two things can happen:
  1. The UML Package URI value is updated with the value from the «Resource» IRI tagged value, OR
  2. A new URI value is generated if the «Resource» IRI tagged value is blank.

- When creating or updating a «Resource» IRI tagged value, the UML Package URI value will be updated to be the same as the IRI tagged value.
- When removing a «Resource» IRI tagged value, two things can happen:
  1. A new value is created using the UML package URI value, OR
  2. A new UML Package URI value is generated when UML Package URI value is blank.

GreenAvailable From 19.0 SP2

When changing an element's name in synchronization with its IRI, you can derive that IRI from the element's name.

Consider the following scenario:

1. Create a class named IRI Name 1.
2. Check the tags and see that the IRI tagged value is IRI-Name1. [ATTACHMENT filename='iri1.PNG']
3. Change the class name to IRI Name 2.
4. The following message appears. [ATTACHMENT filename='iri3.PNG']
5. Click one of the following:
  1. Update IRI . This option will keep the new class name, but change the IRI tagged value to match the new class. [ATTACHMENT filename='iri4.PNG']
  2. Rename Only . This option will keep the new name, but a label will now be added that is the same IRI tagged value as the old name. [ATTACHMENT filename='iri5.PNG']
  3. Cancel . The IRI stays the same and the class name does not change.

1236311766

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeling Semantics' space='MCM2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170478146 space=MCM2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00232: The role of ontologies and reasoners

- page_id: `170478146`
- space_key: `MCM2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCM2024xR1/pages/170478146/The+role+of+ontologies+and+reasoners
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T15:08:53.634+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Concept Modeler Documentation > Introduction
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1152525498

1152525500

1152525499

An ontology is a formal naming and definition of the types, properties, and interrelationship of the entities that exist in some domain. It defines and represents consensual knowledge as a set of concepts within a domain, using a shared vocabulary to denote the types, properties, and relationships of those concepts. Artificial intelligence, the Semantic Web, systems engineering, software engineering, biomedical informatics, library science, enterprise bookmarking, and information architecture all uses ontologies to represent concepts that belong to their domain in very specific ways. Domain ontologies (domain-specific ontologies) plays a significant role in the definition and use of an enterprise architecture framework.

Ontologies are commonly encoded using ontology languages. OWL (Web Ontology Language), produced by the W3C Web Ontology Working Group, is one of the formal languages to construct ontologies. It is an international standard for encoding and sharing ontologies and is designed to support the Semantic Web. An OWL ontology may include classes, relations, attributes, formal axioms, and instances. OWL can be used to build most kinds of ontologies. The Concept Modeler maps to a subset of the OWL. The following are some examples of what you can do with OWL ontologies using the Concept Modeler:

- Import existing OWL ontologies for concept reuse, and/or as a starting point for the creation of a concept model.
- Export a concept model as an OWL ontology, which can be augmented by the addition of axioms not supported by the Concept Modeler. For example, axioms can be added to constrain model interpretations, or for advanced reasoning (e.g., transitivity) not supported by the Concept Modeler and UML.

A semantic reasoner infers logical consequences from a set of asserted axioms in an ontology, and typically provides automated support for reasoning tasks such as classification and querying. The inferences made by a semantic reasoner over an ontology generated by the Concept Modeler can be used to find logical inconsistencies in the primary concept model. Hence, a semantic reasoner can provide information to validate and improve a concept model.

The logical consistency of a concept model is particularly important if the desired result is a system that classifies information. As stated above, the inferences made by a semantic reasoner can help to ensure that the concept model is logically consistent in its classification.

The Concept Modeler maps to a subset of OWL that is most useful to the business purposes of defining a concept model. Consequently, any attempt to “round trip,” (i.e., re-import a possibly modified ontology model that has been exported by the concept model) is very likely to be “lossy”, particularly if the ontology generated from a concept model is augmented by additional axioms not supported by the Concept Modeler. Therefore, as a prime tenet of MDA, the concept model is considered to be the “primary” artifact, and the ontology is the “secondary” artifact. Business concept development and changes must be made in the concept model.

1152525496

**Related pages**

- MDA
- Concept modeling purpose
- Open-world assumption vs. closed-world assumption
- Information modeling purpose

OWL (Web Ontology Language), produced by the W3C Web Ontology Working Group, is one of the formal languages to construct ontologies. The following are some examples of what you can do with OWL ontologies using the Concept Modeler:</p><ul><li>Import existing OWL ontologies for concept reuse, and/or as a starting point for the creation of a concept model.</li><li>Export a concept model as an OWL ontology, which can be augmented by the addition of axioms not supported by the Concept Modeler.
